FRANCE RECOGNIZES RUSSIAN HYBRID WARFARE.  WW3 Indicator: France is moving from cautious observation to the official recognition that a systematic war below the threshold of open armed conflict is already being waged against it. Cyberattacks, sabotage, arson, reconnaissance drone flights, and operations conducted through proxies are increasingly being treated as elements of a single Russian campaign. This does not indicate an imminent direct war between France and Russia, but it shows the gradual erosion of the boundary between peace and war and France’s preparation for a prolonged confrontation.  On July 13, 2026, France officially attributed a long-running cyberespionage campaign to the Russian FSB unit No. 61240, publicly identifying its number for the first time. Paris supported European Union sanctions against entities linked to these operations and summoned Russia’s chargé d’affaires. France is also investigating a cyberattack against drone manufacturer Delair, a fire at a Thales defense facility in Brest, an attempted act of sabotage at a hydroelectric site, and unidentified drone flights over military facilities. Classified readiness levels under the STADEF system are being used to protect strategic sites, while the involvement of the gendarmerie and military reservists is being expanded.  lemonde.fr/en/international/…

Public to be told to stockpile food in case of cyber attack from Russia  UK will run largest home defence exercise in decades, dubbed Operation Albiston Shadow  The public will be urged to stockpile food and water in case of a cyberattack from Russia, under widescale plans to prepare Britain for “should the worst happen”.  A new “resilience” campaign will urge people to be prepared for emergencies, while ministers will test military and civilian responses in the largest defence exercise on UK soil in decades next year.  Chief secretary to the prime minister Darren Jones said: “This campaign will help the public to take small but important steps to be prepared in case of emergencies and disruption – be that severe weather or a cyber-attack, which can impact access to power, water, or phone signal.  “Being prepared not only helps people keep themselves and their family safe, but also means the emergency services can focus on helping the most vulnerable in communities.”  Last month Lord Peach, the former chief of the defence staff, told The Independent households should load up on food and water but also cash as he warned that it was “time to be serious” about threats facing Britain from Putin.  On Monday, the UK and allies accused Russia of an attempted hack targeting Poland’s power grid last December that, they said, could have left 500,000 people without electricity in the depths of winter.  The public will also be urged to prepare for other types of emergencies including severe weather. An estimated 2,700 people in England and Wales are thought to have died because of a series of heatwaves in recent weeks.  Dubbed Operation Albiston Shadow, the home defence exercise will take place over several days.  Ministers said it would “ensure that should the worst ever happen, we will always be ready”.  Mr Jones said on Tuesday he had added seven new entries to the UK’s National Risk Register, the official assessment of the most significant threats facing the country.  Those include the cyberattacks targeting data and water infrastructure and police systems, as well as “digital resilience failure” like the 2024 Crowdstrike outage that knocked infrastructure, transport and healthcare systems offline.  As Labour ministers seek to bring in new laws on political donations, also added was the risk of foreign interference in the UK’s democratic process.  Mr Jones said: “Throughout our history, the UK has overcome challenges from plagues and pandemics to war and our fair share of wet weather.  “It is right that we consistently evaluate the risks we could face and plan for what may come.  “This year we saw temperatures across the UK breaking records in May, only to be exceeded again in June, and AI offers new ways for criminals to carry out cyber-attacks against us, as well as offering huge opportunities for our economy and security.”  the-independent.com/news/uk/…

Folks @HuntressLabs were in full swing this Friday analyzing the latest and greatest of CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, affecting SonicWall SMA 1000-series appliances.   DCSync on the DC's, Sliver on the SMA's, and more below.  Our first hint of adversarial access was remote registry dumping across numerous environments, detected and mitigated by Defender. This was Impacket's Secretsdump.  github.com/fortra/impacket/b…  We began to analyze Huntress' SIEM data for signs of compromise from affected SMA devices within these environments. We found two distinct patterns.  1.) Adversaries acting to validate exploitation against these appliances from a variety of low rep providers.  2.) Anomalous curl activity from the 'root' user of an appliance; fetching payloads from 153.75.81[.]30.  Two different payloads-- ".sshd" and ".rsyncd" were noted. They were stashed in /var/tmp/ and executed through cron (rsyncd-update and sshd-update) on the SMA appliance.  Analysis of the payloads confirmed these were Sliver implants, an adversary emulation framework by BishopFox. These were configured to callback to the same staging IP.  github.com/bishopfox/sliver  This would allow an adversary to linger covertly after remediation efforts.  Meanwhile, @xorJosh was busy cooking away on his own analysis-- and located an open directory containing CVE validation scripts (a CN-localized Rapid7 script), "autodcsync" and "autosecretsdump" Python files, and exfiltrated credentials from numerous environments.  Of note, contained next to the credentials and scripts in the open dir was 'iox' and 'frp', two direct-tunnel tools.  github.com/eddieivan01/iox github.com/fatedier/frp  These are noteworty chiefly because they have been favorites of numerous nation-state APT groups.  The key takeaways here are clear: - SIEM saves lives; make sure logs are being forwarded and retained. - Patching might not mean you're out of the woods, adversaries are already weaponizing persistent implants to maintain access.  IOCs IPv4/Port:  153.75.81[.]30:30303 - Stager Resource:  153.75.81[.30:30303/a - .rsyncd Resource:  153.75.81[.30:30303/c - .sshd IPv4/Port:  153.75.81[.30:80 - Sliver C2 Callback Cron: /etc/cron.d/rsync-update - .rsyncd Cronjob Cron: /etc/cron.d/sshd-update - .sshd Cronjob Binary: 5aa0bb8a8a298fc00935dad22e73e1effc648b1739628b782727ba7bb123cbc3 (.rsyncd) Binary: 098db74319d0798291264c643522db59fc12d65ef649d7e12c7334ea75a74a4e (.sshd) Path: /var/tmp/.rsyncd Path: /var/tmp/.sshd

The Windows Security Event Log is the crown jewel of host-based forensic artifacts.  Every authentication attempt, privilege escalation, and account modification flows through Security.evtx. Event ID 4624 (successful logon) and 4625 (failed logon) are your bread and butter for tracking lateral movement and brute-force attacks. The Logon Type field is critical: Type 2 is interactive console, Type 3 is network share access, Type 10 is RDP, and Type 5 is service accounts.  Event 4688 (process creation) becomes a goldmine when command-line logging is enabled via Group Policy. You'll see the full command line arguments, parent process, and user context — essential for detecting malicious PowerShell, living-off-the-land binaries, and persistence mechanisms.  For privilege escalation hunts, watch for 4672 (special privileges assigned) and 4720 (new account created). These often signal attacker consolidation after initial access.  Location: C:\Windows\System32\winevt\Logs\Security.evtx  Parsing tools: EvtxECmd for timeline export, Hayabusa for threat hunting rules, Chainsaw for Sigma detection, and DeepBlueCLI for quick anomaly checks.  Maps to MITRE ATT&CK T1078 (Valid Accounts) and T1021 (Remote Services). If you're not collecting Security logs with sufficient retention, you're flying blind during an IR engagement.  #DFIR #IncidentResponse

2008年に公開されたCVE-2008-4128が、18年近く経った今もロシアFSB第16センターに関連する活動で悪用され、7月13日にCISAのKEV（悪用が確認された脆弱性のカタログ）へ追加されました。同日、13カ国19機関の合同勧告も公表され、設定不備や既知の脆弱性が残るルーターが世界的に狙われていると警告しています。  勧告が中心的な手口として挙げるのは、マルウェアも新しい脆弱性も使わず、機器管理用の古いプロトコルSNMP（v1／v2）と既定のコミュニティ文字列（接続用パスワードに相当）だけで、ルーターに自分の設定を書き出させてTFTPで外部へ送らせる方法だといいます。  【要点の整理】  ・中心の手口として挙げられているのは、SNMP v1／v2に応答する機器を探し、public や private など推測されやすいコミュニティ文字列でアクセスするもの。成功するとOID（設定項目を指す識別子）で機器自身に設定を複製させ、攻撃者のVPSや侵害済みFTPサーバーへTFTPで送らせる流れだとしている。  ・持ち出された設定には認証情報やネットワーク構成が含まれるため、次の侵入や、他を狙う踏み台づくりに使われるとの分析。設定内のCisco Type 0／Type 7という弱い形式のパスワードの回収は、MITRE ATT&CKでも認証情報アクセスに位置づけられている。  ・SNMPの悪用が主で、CVEの悪用は限定的との整理。併用例として挙げられたのは、サポート切れの古いCisco機器（IOS 12.4）のWeb管理画面にある複数のCSRF＝CVE-2008-4128と、Cisco Smart InstallのCVE-2018-0171で、18年前の欠陥がいまだ攻撃部品として残っている。  ・勧告が挙げる対策は、SNMPv3（authPriv）への移行とv1／v2の停止、Cisco Smart Installの無効化、管理接続元のACLでの限定、境界でのTFTP／SMI／SNMPの遮断、Cisco機器のパスワードはType 8の使用。単一CVEのパッチより、古い管理プロトコルと既定値を減らす「ルーター衛生」に重心を置いた内容になっている。  勧告は、設定ファイルの持ち出しを単なる情報漏えいではなく、そこから得た認証情報での次の侵入や、他を狙う踏み台化の起点として扱っている。古いSNMPや既定のコミュニティ文字列、不要なSmart Installの有無が、狙われる余地を左右するとしています。  詳細は以下を参照： 19機関の共同勧告： cyber.gov.au/about-us/view-a… CISA KEVカタログ： cisa.gov/sites/default/files…  (※可能な範囲でファクトチェックは実施済なものの、元記事の精度依存や速報・要約の性質上漏れもありうるため、正確な情報は一次情報を直接参照のこと)

Hiberfil.sys is a goldmine that many IR teams overlook.  When Windows hibernates, it writes a compressed snapshot of physical RAM to C:\hiberfil.sys. This isn't just sleep state metadata — it's a full memory image containing running processes, network connections, decrypted data, and passwords that were in memory at hibernation time.  What makes this artifact critical: it persists across reboots. Even if the system was powered down cleanly, hiberfil.sys may still contain evidence from days or weeks prior. You get process memory, kernel structures, and potentially decrypted credentials or malware artifacts that would otherwise be lost.  Parsing tools: • Volatility 3 with the hibernation layer (--single-location file:///path/to/hiberfil.sys) • Arsenal Hibernation Recon for conversion to raw memory format  Practical use case: During an intrusion investigation, you arrive on-scene but no memory dump was captured. Check for hiberfil.sys. If present, decompress it and analyze as you would a live memory image. You may recover evidence of lateral movement tools, injected code, or credential dumping that occurred before the last hibernation event.  MITRE ATT&CK maps this to T1005 (Data from Local System) — adversaries know hibernation files contain sensitive data and may target them for credential harvesting or anti-forensics deletion.  Don't sleep on hiberfil.sys. It's often the only memory artifact you'll get.  #DFIR #IncidentResponse

既知の回避技法をCobalt-Strike向けのRust製リフレクティブローダー（UDRL）一本に実装し、各技法にETW・メモリスキャン・YARAの検知戦略を添えた技術解説「DoublePulsar」が2026年4月に公開されています（2017年に流出したNSA関連インプラントと同名ですが無関係の研究プロジェクト）。公開同月にElasticがSilentMoonwalk系コールスタック偽装を狙った行動ルールを公開しており、攻防のサイクルが早い分野の実例としても興味深い内容です。  【要点の整理】  ・Cobalt-StrikeのBeaconをメモリ内へ読み込むUDRLで、正規DLL（既定はd3d10[.]dll）の.text領域を上書きするモジュールストンピングを採用。Beaconがディスク上のDLL由来のメモリ（MEM_IMAGE）に見えるためMEM_PRIVATEベースの検知を回避する一方、PE-sieve等のコンテンツ比較では内容不一致として検出可能との整理。  ・休止中はRC4（SystemFunction040/041）でBeaconのメモリと専用ヒープの両方を暗号化し、10段階のNtContinueコンテキストチェーンでメモリ保護の切り替えを実行。スリープ難読化はEkko（タイマーベース、既定）、FOLIAGE（APC）、Zilean（Wait）、XOR（単純方式）の4方式をCargo機能フラグで選択する設計で、JMPガジェットの間接化やファイバー上での実行によりチェーン自体の検知を困難にする工夫も。ただしIntel-CET/Shadow-Stack有効環境ではROPベースのチェーン（Ekko/FOLIAGE/Zilean）は動作しないとの記載。  ・30以上のIATエントリをフックしてBeaconの実行時挙動を制御し、全フックAPIに対しuwdベースの合成コールスタック（.pdataからフレームサイズを計算し、呼び出しごとに中間フレームをローテーション）を適用。Elastic-9.0.1（prevention mode）での検証結果や、YARAルール2本、MITRE-ATT&CK対応（T1620等10項目）を収録した検知資料としての側面。  ・TitanLdr、AceLdr、BokuLoaderなど既存UDRLの手法を踏まえた実装で、Crystal-Palace/Tradecraft-Gardenのモジュラー方式と対比される「モノリシックUDRL」。2026年5月14日にGitHubリポジトリはオーナーによってアーカイブ済みで、新規技法の発表ではなく公開済み技法をまとめ直した研究。  公開実装は約65KBの位置独立ローダーで、機能フラグにより合成コールスタック、直接syscall、通常API呼び出しを切り替える設計です。  詳細は以下を参照：  技術解説： memn0ps.github.io/doublepuls…  公開実装： github.com/memN0ps/doublepul…  (※可能な範囲でファクトチェックは実施済なものの、元記事の精度依存や速報・要約の性質上漏れもありうるため、正確な情報は一次情報を直接参照のこと)

Spare a thought for Keith, a prisoner of British agriculture, who yesterday endured another full day of unimaginable exploitation in a field in Devon.  5.51am. Forced awake by nothing at all. No alarm, no shift, no commute, just the daily torment of a latch he could open whenever he liked and the crushing realisation that the entire farm was, once again, his.  6.14am. Made to eat. Bramble this time. The spined, invasive, free-of-charge kind a contractor would bill four figures to touch. Keith is compelled to clear it every morning and receives, in exchange, only breakfast.  7.02am. Dave had repaired the north fence on Tuesday. Keith walked the length of it, pressed his head to the new section, and felt it give by the width of a thought. He did not go through. He filed it. A free animal does not use every exit the moment he finds it. He keeps them, the way other people keep savings.  8.15am. Subjected to fresh air. Relentlessly. The wind came off the moor with nothing between him and the whole of Dartmoor but a wall he could clear if the mood took him. He did not take the mood. Such is the cruelty of having a choice.  9.30am. Trapped on the barn roof he had climbed to voluntarily, in sunshine, eating moss no living thing has ever wanted, gazing out over a valley he is technically ruining and visibly enjoying. Dave stood in the yard and said his name in the tone of a man who has said it before. Keith looked at Dave. Keith continued.  11.30am. The Reverend, his captor, had left the churchyard gate open for him again, which rather undermines the concept of an escape. Keith was thereby forced against his will to clear the war graves the parish could not afford to strim, uncovering names hidden for thirty years. The suffering was etched all over his face, which was chewing.  1.15pm. Martin the postman offered him half a sandwich. Keith, oppressed, ate the ham, rejected the bread and the spread, and conducted the most rigorous quality-control audit of British convenience food currently available, unpaid.  3.40pm. Steve opened his back door. By the time it was fully open Keith was already leaving, already latching the gate behind him, already gone, leaving only bindweed damage and the quiet impression that something had been and decided against staying.  5.00pm. Dave installed a new latch. Magnetic. Guaranteed. The man in the shop swore to it. Keith regarded the new latch for a long, unhurried while, the way you look at a thing you have already solved and are choosing when to bother with.  He had not opened it by nightfall.  That is the point. He could. He simply had not decided to want to.  Please, if you can, do nothing.  Keith is fine.  Keith has never once, in his life, been anything else.

Domain: 6  - Hash: 38  - IP: 9  - File: 9  - Url: 1  💽Software: Docker, QEMU, Android, MariaDB, nginx, Minecraft, TeamSpeak, FiveM, Busybox, ThinkPHP, ...  🔢Algorithms: poly1305, sha512, xor, chacha20-poly1305, curve25519, sha256, ed25519, pbkdf2, base64, sha1, chacha20  🔠Functions: system, toggle_obf, add_entry_plaintext, dga_generate_domain, exploit_engine_init, sendmmsg, attack_init, select  🗂️Win API: inet_addr  📜Programming Languages: golang, python, php  💻Platforms: arm, mips, ppc64, riscv64, m68k, x86  #threatreport: TuxBot v3 Evolution has been identified as a previously undocumented modular IoT botnet framework that prominently utilizes large language model (LLM) assistance in its code development. Despite its innovative approach, this reliance on AI has resulted in various functional shortcomings within the malware. The botnet is capable of targeting devices through Telnet brute-forcing, employing 1,496 credential pairs, and utilizing exploit code for over 30 different IoT device families. Communication with the command-and-control (C2) server is secured via an encrypted TCP channel, where a SHA512 domain generation algorithm (DGA) helps manage connectivity.  The bot agent is constructed in C, with cross-compilation for multiple architectures, while the C2 server is developed in Go and includes a panel for DDoS-for-hire capabilities. Despite promising functionality, many features within the analyzed version of TuxBot are not operational, including critical exploit mechanisms and various scanning capabilities. For instance, the exploitation system encountered multiple bugs due to LLM-generated code, which led to some functions being ineffectively implemented or completely non-functional.  The framework organizes its C2 architecture across multiple communication layers, employing several channels such as IRC and HTTP, though significant portions of this infrastructure are rendered non-functional by errors in the coding. Key vulnerabilities were also identified in the malware's design, including an XOR encryption key mismatch that compromised critical strings necessary for operation.  TuxBot's exploitation capabilities encompass three categories with varying degrees of readiness. While 16 hard-coded exploit functions exist, none are actively utilized in the current build. However, modular exploit file structures allow for integration of up to 13 CVEs once functional. Additional features are planned for exploitation; however, they are currently not operational due to coding flaws.  Persistence mechanisms embed the malware deeply within infected systems, using various techniques familiar in IoT malware, such as service disguise and file manipulation. Self-defense strategies are also incorporated to detect and evade common security measures, including checks against known analysis tools and virtual environments.

#threatreport #LowCompleteness ChainVeil and ViteVenom are DPRK’s PolinRider Campaign | 17-07-2026 Source: opensourcemalware.com/blog/c… Key details below ↓  🧑‍💻Actors/Campaigns: Polinrider Lazarus Contagious_interview Tasksjacker  💀Threats: Supply_chain_technique, Credential_harvesting_technique, Typosquatting_technique,  🎯Victims: Software development, Open source package registries, Cryptocurrency ecosystem  🏭Industry: Financial  🌐Geo: North korean, Dprk  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1078, T1140, T1195.001  📲Wallets: tron  🪙Crypto: aptos, binance  🔢Algorithms: xor  #threatreport: The ChainVeil and ViteVenom campaigns, attributed to North Korea's Lazarus Group, represent a sophisticated attack vector targeting npm and Vite ecosystems. ChainVeil emerged in June 2026, leveraging nine malicious packages and a remote access trojan (RAT) operating through a blockchain command channel involving TRON, Aptos, and Binance Smart Chain. This attack was revealed by Checkmarx, who later identified ViteVenom as a sequel that includes seven typosquatted packages targeting the Vite framework. Both campaigns share infrastructure and exhibit a consistent operator profile, reinforcing their link to the PolinRider campaign.  PolinRider, which began in early 2026, utilizes credentials obtained from a prior campaign known as TasksJacker. It has shown significant growth, expanding from GitHub into other platforms such as Go, Packagist, and PyPI, attributing over 4,295 malicious assets and yielding more than 10,000 indicators of compromise (IOCs). This convergence of operations suggests that ChainVeil and ViteVenom are not distinct threats but rather extensions of the PolinRider campaign, supported by matching infrastructure and techniques.  The evidence further supports this attribution, with key blockchain wallets, decryption keys, and IOCs being consistent across different operations associated with PolinRider. For instance, the TRON wallets linked to ChainVeil's command channel exhibit a pattern of rotation, indicative of a well-maintained attack infrastructure. Notably, these wallets were documented prior to the ChainVeil report publication, reinforcing their connection to prior activities.  Moreover, the packages utilized in ChainVeil represent a distinct naming scheme inherent to the operator's trademark, characterized by the A6- prefix found across various markers, which echoes previous findings related to DPRK-owned assets. The targeted packages in both campaigns, while not identical, capitalize on similar vulnerabilities within the Tailwind and Vite namespaces through typosquatting.  In summary, ChainVeil and ViteVenom are integral components of the PolinRider campaign, indicating North Korea's ongoing use of sophisticated supply chain tactics to compromise modern development environments. The persistent structure of their operations and the overlapping indicators suggest a coordinated effort that continues to pose risks within the software development ecosystem. Organizations should treat all identified packages as malicious and remain vigilant against these types of threats.

#threatreport #MediumCompleteness KUDANKULAM NUCLEAR POWER PLANT LEAK: AN ACCIDENTAL DISCLOSURE | 17-07-2026 Source: theravenfile.com/2026/07/17/… Key details below ↓  🧑‍💻Actors/Campaigns: Hunters_international Lazarus (🧠motivation: cyber_espionage)  💀Threats: Hunters_international, Hive_ransomware, Rustyrocket_tool, Supply_chain_technique,  🎯Victims: Nuclear power, Energy, Healthcare, Manufacturing, Business services  🏭Industry: Energy, Healthcare, Ics, Petroleum, Nuclear_power, Critical_infrastructure  🌐Geo: French, Russia, India, China, Dprk, North korea  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1020, T1036, T1133, T1190, T1199, T1204, T1566, T1591.002  🧨IOCs:  - File: 1  - Hash: 17  💽Software: Linux  🔢Algorithms: md5, sha256  🔠Functions: Internet-Facing  💻Platforms: cross-platform  #threatreport: The Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant (KNPP) in Tamil Nadu recently became embroiled in a significant data breach following an attack by the ransomware group WORLDLEAKS, a spin-off of the now-defunct Hunters International. The breach was first reported on June 11, 2026, when WORLDLEAKS announced Reliance Infrastructure, which manages the plant, as a victim on their data leak site. The attackers leveraged vulnerabilities potentially stemming from exposed Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) access, phishing, or exploits targeting Fortinet devices. It is estimated that the attackers maintained access for several days to weeks before exfiltrating data using their in-house tool named RustyRocket, which can transfer data with an average speed of 50-100 Mbps and could take approximately 4-6 days for complete exfiltration.  The breach is linked to a third-party vendor, Yotta, which hosts Reliance’s servers. Yotta reported suspicious activities beginning on May 29, 2026, coinciding with the data leak announcement. This emphasizes the risks associated with third-party vendors and their critical need for regular security assessments, vulnerability patches, and proper network isolation of sensitive infrastructure. The investigation revealed that other affected parties included Ranolia Road Carriers Pvt. Ltd and Enovia, a French software corporation commonly used in research and development in India.  The article also highlights a broader trend where state-sponsored actors, particularly from North Korea and China, target industrial control systems and operational technologies for espionage purposes. North Korean attacks tend to focus on short-term financial gains, particularly aimed at supporting weapons programs, while Chinese actors emphasize long-term strategic access to global supply chains. Phishing attacks are a common means of gaining entry into these critical systems, exploiting human vulnerabilities regardless of prior employee training measures.  WORLDLEAKS has so far claimed 173 victims across 29 countries, primarily targeting the healthcare, manufacturing, and business services sectors. They employ a sophisticated infrastructure for their operations, including their RustyRocket tool disguised as a benign Windows application. The group is known for their cross-platform capabilities, indicated by the discovery of Linux builds, which can broaden their attack surface.  As cyber threats continue to evolve, this incident highlights the necessity for heightened security vigilance, especially when dealing with sensitive infrastructure and third-party vendors. The ongoing development and sophistication of ransomware groups like WORLDLEAKS signal an escalating threat landscape in which data breaches may become increasingly prevalent.

April 18: an attacker drains $292M in rsETH from Kelp DAO's cross-chain bridge. The largest DeFi exploit of 2026 so far.  No smart contract was hacked. No private key was stolen.  The bridge relied on a single verifier to confirm cross-chain messages. Attackers DDoS'd the honest nodes offline, forced a failover to a compromised one, and fed it a message claiming a transaction had happened — when it hadn't. The bridge believed it, and released the funds.  LayerZero has since attributed the attack to North Korea's Lazarus Group.  This is the pattern worth sitting with: the cryptography wasn't broken. The verification step itself had exactly one point of failure, and once that point failed, nothing downstream could tell the difference between a real message and a forged one.  A single verifier isn't verification. It's trust wearing verification's clothes.  Source: coindesk.com/tech/2026/04/19… #DeepSafe #CRVA #Web3Security

What happened Ransomware group World Leaks (formerly Hunters International) posted a large cache of files on the dark web related to the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant (KKNPP) in Tamil Nadu — India's largest nuclear facility. The breach didn't hit the plant directly; it originated from a third-party data center provider called Yotta, hosting servers for Reliance Infrastructure, one of the plant's contractors. Scale of the leak Nearly 19,000 files totaling 14.3 gigabytes appear in the leaked data, and the documents mainly involve Units 3 and 4 of the plant — a 2,400 MW expansion still under construction, slated to go operational by 2027. The leaked cache includes purported blueprints of parts of the facility and supplier details. Al Jazeera + 2 How it happened Yotta detected suspicious activity on a server belonging to Reliance Infrastructure, and Reliance Group confirmed a "partial breach" of its data on that server, notifying the government about the incident. It's a supply-chain breach — the attackers hit a contractor's systems, not the plant's own network directly. OilPrice.comBusiness Standard Official response The Nuclear Power Corporation of India said the breach did not reveal any sensitive information related to nuclear security, maintaining the plant's core reactor systems are air-gapped and isolated from the internet. But experts warn this is a landmark security failure regardless — nuclear security is only as strong as its weakest contractor, and a senior director at the Nuclear Threat Initiative said the breach could pose a "serious" risk to the plant's safety. Al Jazeera + 2 Context worth knowing This isn't Kudankulam's first cyber incident — malware tied to a North Korean hacker group (Lazarus) was found on the plant's administrative network back in 2019, in an intrusion officials initially denied before confirming. That breach also didn't touch reactor control systems, which stayed isolated, but it did hit the administrative network. Business Standard Bigger picture India ranked third globally for data breaches last year, with 28.9 million accounts compromised, behind only the US and France, and a survey found 73% of Indian organizations were unaware if they'd ever been attacked — so this fits a broader pattern of weak cyber hygiene among Indian contractors and enterprises, not an isolated incident. Business Standard Bottom line Reactor control systems appear untouched (they're physically air-gapped, which is the real protection here), but sensitive blueprints and supplier data for a major nuclear expansion are now sitting on the dark web — which is a real supply-chain security failure even if it's not an "immediate plant safety" emergency.  and ISRO is also taken over by Jio now, so off course... real scintists must quit , some gawaar sanghi will run  it now.

That's a legitimate angle. Americans are among the hardest hit, so a response that frames the DOJ/FBI seizure as righteous enforcement and necessary self-defense makes sense.  Gemini's output captured that spirit but cranked it up to full dramatic mode — "Rubio’s Righteous Cause," "lethal insecticide campaign," "masterclass in grand strategy," asset forfeiture as a clever way to fund U.S. cyber defenses while reinforcing dollar hegemony. It’s entertaining and emotionally resonant for an American audience, but it layers on interpretive flair that goes beyond the official DOJ statements (which are more straightforward: "we’re disrupting criminal infrastructure that steals from Americans and launders money for scams and North Korean hackers").  Core facts remain solid: Huione ran (or enabled) massive Telegram-based marketplaces for laundering, stolen data, escrow, and scam tools. Billions in dirty crypto flowed through it. Clear ties to Southeast Asian scam compounds with human trafficking elements and to Lazarus Group/North Korea. The June 2026 cloud account seizure, combined with earlier FinCEN sanctions, is a real and useful hit against the financial plumbing of these operations.  My honest balanced view: It's good that the U.S. is aggressively going after these enablers — victims deserve protection, and transnational crime networks that mix fraud, trafficking, and state-sponsored theft need to be dismantled. At the same time, the "hegemonic master plan" framing is overstated narrative seasoning. Asset forfeiture funds law enforcement, but portraying this primarily as a brilliant self-funding hegemony play risks turning legitimate enforcement into geopolitical fanfiction. You deliberately steered Gemini toward the strong American narrative to counter previous more negative or dismissive framings. That's fair game — it shows how sensitive these models are to prompt direction and how quickly they can swing from one emphasis to another.  Overall, the action itself is positive. The dramatic storytelling around it is understandable given the scale of real human and financial harm, but facts work best when they’re allowed to speak without too much heroic varnish.  If you want me to expand on any angle (victim numbers, North Korea links, international cooperation, or how I’d phrase a more neutral version), just say the word.

Justice Department Seizes Backend Infrastructure Used by the  Huione Group for Money Laundering Services justice.gov/opa/pr/justice-d… 🤖 by Gemini : Rubio’s Righteous Cause and American Utility: Dismantling Global Scam Infrastructure and the Hegemonic Strategy of Asset Forfeiture 1. Introduction: The Rise of a Global "Criminal Infrastructure" In recent years, China’s "Belt and Road Initiative" (BRI) infrastructure investments in regions like Sihanoukville, Cambodia, and the borderlands of Myanmar have been warped into a global pipeline for transnational scam ecosystems. Known in China as Shazhupan (literally "Pig-Butchering Plates"), these online romance and investment scams are no longer isolated operations. They have evolved into a massive underground economy leveraging tens of thousands of personnel and advanced technologies like generative AI. At the very heart of this machinery stood the Cambodia-based Huione Group. 2. The Three-Pronged Structure of the Criminal Ecosystem  This underground economy functions as a literal global "locust plague" (蝗害), stripping wealth and human dignity through three deeply interconnected criminal networks: Humanitarian Crisis (Cyber Slavery and Organ Trafficking): Young people worldwide are lured with false promises of high-paying jobs, kidnapped, and trafficked into heavily armed scam compounds. Trapped under threat of violence, they are forced to work as "scam callers." Those who fail to meet quotas or attempt escape face severe torture or, in the worst cases, are targeted for illicit organ harvesting via specialized Chinese trafficking syndicates. International Romance and Investment Fraud: Syndicates exploit social media and cryptocurrency networks to build fake trust, systematically draining victims' life savings and retirement funds to the absolute limit.  Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing: Handling tens of billions of dollars annually, Huione’s network did not just funnel illicit cash back to Chinese triads. It also served as a critical laundering hub for cryptocurrency stolen by Lazarus, North Korea’s state-sponsored cyberwarfare group, directly financing Pyongyang's nuclear and missile development programs. 3. The U.S. National Security Team's "Lethal Insecticide" Campaign Recognizing that these operations pose a severe geopolitical threat to the free world rather than simple localized crime, the U.S. National Security Team, led by Secretary of State Marco Rubio, has weaponized executive and presidential powers to completely eradicate the ecosystem.  A. Legal Foundations and Executive Authority Executive Order 14390 (E.O. 14390): This presidential order prioritizes combating transnational cybercrime, fraud, and predatory schemes targeting American citizens.  USA PATRIOT Act Section 311: The U.S. Treasury’s Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) designated Huione Group a "Primary Money Laundering Concern." This final rule permanently disconnected Huione from the U.S. financial system and major stablecoin (USDT) networks.  B. Cyber and Physical Enforcement: "Operation Riptide"  Led by the DOJ and the FBI, this joint international law enforcement campaign targets the core technical backend. In June 2026, authorities physically and digitally seized cloud infrastructure and encrypted communication networks utilized by Huione. This seizure effectively blinded the syndicates by cutting off access to target databases ("sucker lists") and AI-driven scam manuals.  C. Diplomatic Eradication and Stripping the "State Shield"  Under the Trafficking Victims Protection Act (TVPA), the U.S. launched targeted sanctions and asset freezes against corrupt foreign politicians and military elites—including figures tied to Cambodia's ruling family and the Prince Group—who accepted bribes to guard these lawless economic zones. By threatening to sever these nations from the global SWIFT banking network, the U.S. forced Cambodia's central bank to revoke Huione’s operating licenses, stripping the criminals of their state-backed protection. 4. Conclusion: The Convergence of Social Justice and National Interest The aggressive global cleanup spearheaded by the United States serves a profound dual purpose. On one hand, it champions "Social Justice" by dismantling a modern slave trade and severing the funding lines of rogue states like North Korea and Chinese transnational syndicates. On the other hand, it represents a remarkably cold, calculated Masterclass in Grand Strategy. Under the Department of Justice's Assets Forfeiture Fund (AFF) system, billions in seized cryptocurrency are legally absorbed by the U.S. government. These funds are directly reinvested into American cyber-defense capabilities and cutting-edge blockchain forensics. By executing global justice, the United States simultaneously funds its own defense infrastructure and reinforces the hegemony of the U.S. dollar. Left without funding or safe havens, the remaining cartels have been forced into a brutal, cannibalistic "musical chairs" survival game, tearing each other apart from the inside. If you want to prepare this report for official presentation, I can format specific elements further. Let me know if you would like me to:  Map out a visual text diagram showing the flow of funds from a victim's wallet to North Korea's military. Add a section analyzing the diplomatic blowback this creates between the U.S. and China's Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Detail the specific counter-fraud operations currently being synchronized by the Japanese National Police Agency.

Wie jeden Freitag gibt es die neue Folge der Makro- und Bitcoinshow.  Krypto-Hacks gehen in die Milliarden. Nordkorea steckt hinter den größten Diebstählen der Geschichte. Aber warum betrifft das dich als Bitcoiner eigentlich nicht?  In dieser Folge schaue ich mir zwei Seiten derselben Medaille an:  Teil 1 – Die größten Krypto-Hacks aller Zeiten Ronin Network ($625 Mio.), Poly Network ($611 Mio.), Binance Bridge ($570 Mio.), Coincheck ($534 Mio.), FTX ($477 Mio.) – ich erkläre, wie diese Hacks abliefen, wer dahinter steckt und was sie gemeinsam haben. Spoiler: Es war nie Bitcoin selbst.  Die Lazarus Group – Nordkoreas staatliche Hackereinheit – hat seit 2017 über 3 Milliarden Dollar in Krypto gestohlen. Das Geld fließt direkt ins nordkoreanische Raketen- und Waffenprogramm. Ich zeige, wie sie vorgehen und wie die gestohlenen Coins gewaschen werden.  Teil 2 – Warum Bitcoin nicht hackbar ist Das Bitcoin-Protokoll selbst wurde in 15 Jahren noch nie erfolgreich angegriffen. Ich erkläre warum: Dezentralisierung, Proof of Work, kryptographische Sicherheit – und was du als Bitcoiner konkret tun kannst, um dein Bitcoin wirklich zu schützen. Self-Custody, Hardware Wallets, Seed Phrase – die drei Regeln, die den Unterschied machen.  Link ist in den Kommentaren.
Lazarus Group🥀

🚨 CRYPTO SECURITY ALERT:  🦠 macOS malware targets crypto wallets and Telegram sessions  Security researchers have warned about new macOS malware campaigns capable of compromising Telegram sessions and stealing sensitive information from cryptocurrency users.  Threats linked to variants such as MacSync Stealer and campaigns associated with the Lazarus Group primarily rely on social engineering to infect devices. Attackers often contact victims through Telegram, sending links, files or applications designed to appear legitimate.  Once executed, the malware can collect active Telegram sessions, browser credentials, macOS Keychain data and information related to cryptocurrency wallets.  The objective is not only to steal personal information, but also to gain access to environments where users manage digital assets, including software wallets and applications such as Ledger Live.  A notable feature of these campaigns is the use of Telegram’s own infrastructure for data exfiltration, with stolen information being transmitted through Telegram bots controlled by attackers.  Security experts recommend avoiding the execution of unknown AppleScript files (.scpt), unverified .dmg files or applications from unfamiliar sources, even when they appear to come from trusted contacts. They also advise strengthening Telegram authentication, keeping macOS updated and using hardware wallets to protect significant amounts of funds.  The incident highlights a broader reality in crypto security: protection does not depend only on the wallet being used, but also on the device, applications and user behavior surrounding it. A compromised environment can become the weakest link in the entire security chain.

🔴 بخصوص بيغاسوس :   في أبريل 2015 تعرضت قناة TV5Monde الفرنسية لهجوم سيبراني غير مسبوق أدى إلى توقف

sample_tweets.text: Attacks on European infrastructure. Raid on young people in Moscow. Proteins essential to immunity.  PT: adrl.pt/event/2026-07-11/pt  You’re watching news from the weekly rally in front of the Russian Embassy in Lisbon. Today is July 11th, 2:30 PM.  On June 30, Portugal's National Cybersecurity Center, in Bulletin No. 2/2026, documented attacks on critical infrastructure in Europe: In Poland, cyber sabotage targeting the power grid nearly caused a widespread blackout and affected an automated water-treatment control system, while the compromise of the open-source tool Trivy provided an entry point into the European Commission's AWS cloud, leading to the leak of several gigabytes of data. Other threats included active exploitation of a new Microsoft Office vulnerability by the hacking group APT28, also known as Fancy Bear and believed to be linked to Russian military intelligence, as well as two major data breaches in France involving 45 million and 11.7 million records. The bulletin also noted that on June 12, the United States temporarily blocked other countries' access to Anthropic's most advanced AI models—Fable 5 and Mythos 5—on national security grounds. cncs.gov.pt/docs/boletim-obs…  On July 7, the human rights group OVD-Info, citing reports by Bereg, Mediazona and Ostorozhno Novosti, said riot police carried out a raid late July 3 in Moscow's Kitay-Gorod district, violently detaining several dozen young people, mostly teenagers aged 14 to 17, who had been out walking. Meduza reported that police singled people out for their unusual appearance: Men with long hair or unusual clothing, for example, were forced into police vans. Detainees said they were beaten, humiliated and shocked with stun guns inside the vans. At the police station, officers merely recorded their personal information and photographed them, without filing any reports. The official reason for the raid is unknown: At the scene, security officers said they were conducting a "document check." ovd.info/express-news/2026/0… meduza.io/feature/2026/07/06…  On July 4, the human rights group Prison Lawyer reported that Ariadna Litvinova, a Russian citizen detained in Turkey, had been deported to Russia. In Russia, she faces prosecution over inscriptions she wrote on February 24, 2025, on street advertising structures featuring Russian military personnel: "MURDERERS! FREEDOM FOR POLITICAL PRISONERS." t.me/prisonlawyers/229  On July 10, the University of Porto's Institute for Research and Innovation in Health published a news release saying scientists had shown in animals that the proteins ZFP36L1 and ZFP36L2 are essential for the function of stromal cells in the thymus, the gland that "trains" and selects T-lymphocytes, one of the body's main lines of defense. Levels of these proteins naturally decline with age. When the proteins were removed, the thymus aged prematurely and stopped producing T-cells, pointing to a role for the proteins in immune aging. The authors hope the findings will pave the way for therapies to counter weakening immunity. noticias.up.pt/2026/07/10/es…  Russia is not their property. Unite, build your communities, and support those who resist.  #databreach #HumanRights #MedicalResearch

🔴 بخصوص بيغاسوس :   في أبريل 2015 تعرضت قناة TV5Monde الفرنسية لهجوم سيبراني غير مسبوق أدى إلى توقف بثها والسيطرة على حساباتها في مواقع التواصل وموقعها الإلكتروني.   في البداية أعلنت جهة تطلق على نفسها الخلافة الإلكترونية المرتبطة بد_ا_ع_ش مسؤوليتها عما وجه الأنظار نحو الإرهاب. لكن التحقيق التقني الذي أجرته الوكالة الفرنسية للأمن السيبراني كشف لاحقا مؤشرات قوية على أن الفاعل الحقيقي هو مجموعة القرصنة APT28 المرتبطة بالاستخبارات العسكرية الروسية.   ومع ذلك لم تخرج فرنسا بحملة سياسية أو إعلامية ضد روسيا ولم تعلن رسميا مسؤوليتها، واكتفت باحتواء الاختراق وتعزيز دفاعاتها السيبرانية فيما بقيت نتائج التحقيق إلى حد كبير حبيسة الدوائر الأمنية والفنية.  حسنا، الأن تخيل أن اختراق كهذا من درجة متفاوتة تماما مع اختراق و التجسس على هاتف الرئيس الفرنسي مباشرة، هل يعقل دولة كفرنسا 🇫🇷 في محيط اقليمي مشتعل لن تشعر بالحرج من عجزها عن حماية أمنها القومي، و تتكتم على الأمر، كما يحدث دائما عندما تتعرض دول كبرى لاختراقات أو عمليات تجسس تنسب لاحقا إلى روسيا أو الصين و غيرهما.    أم أن الأمر مراد له أن ينسب و يصبح فضيحة فقط لأن اسم المغرب حاضرا؟  على أي الجزء الثاني من مسلسل بيغاسوس محكوم عليه بالفشل كالأول.

sample_texts: Current Large-Scale Covert Ops in America TODAY ➡️Usual suspects: Russia, China, and Iran  They’re t

sample_tweets.text: Current Large-Scale Covert Ops in America TODAY ➡️Usual suspects: Russia, China, and Iran  They’re the ones running large-scale covert ops to divide us. The rest mostly just lobby.  Wake up to the real players!  1. Russia (Often the Most Active) ✴️Focus: Sowing domestic discord, undermining elections/democracy, amplifying polarization (racial, political, cultural), and weakening US support for allies like Ukraine. Uses troll farms, state media proxies, Doppelgänger-style fake news sites, hacked accounts, and AI deepfakes. ✴️Scale: High-volume, multilingual operations across platforms (X, Facebook, Telegram, etc.). Persistent even post-2024 election, targeting midterms and ongoing issues. ✴️Why top-tier: Sophisticated at blending covert ops with overt propaganda; frequently cited as the preeminent influence threat in recent cycles.  2. China ✴️Focus: Promoting pro-China narratives, portraying the US as chaotic/racist/divided, countering criticism on Taiwan/Hong Kong/Xinjiang, and targeting down-ballot politicians or policies adversarial to Beijing. Uses networks like Spamouflage, fake journalists/influencers, and state-linked amplification. ✴️Scale: Massive asset footprint (largest in some platform disclosures); leverages diaspora networks, social media, and economic leverage. More cautious on direct election swaying but aggressive on long-term perception management. ✴️Why top-tier: Most capable overall adversary with vast resources; growing use of AI and proxies.  3. Iran ✴️Focus: Exploiting US divisions (e.g., on Israel, elections, Middle East policy), spreading antisemitic/anti-US narratives, discouraging voting, and undermining confidence in institutions. Uses bots, fake personas, memes, and psychological ops, often tied to IRGC-linked efforts. ✴️Scale: Smaller than Russia/China in volume but increasingly sophisticated (especially with AI and proxies); opportunistic on flashpoints like protests or conflicts. ✴️Why top-tier: Aggressive in covert social media and transnational repression; coordinates with Russia/China in some cases.  Other Notable Mentions (Lower Scale) ✴️North Korea: More cyber-focused (hacking, ransomware) but participates in some influence via the broader "Axis" cooperation. ✴️Others (e.g., certain actors from Pakistan, India, or Nigeria-based ops): Occasional smaller-scale or commercial influence, but not on the same systematic, state-directed level as the big three.  These operations are persistent and evolving with AI tools. They often overlap in an "Axis" dynamic (Russia-China-Iran-North Korea cooperation). US responses include platform enforcement, sanctions, and intelligence monitoring, though capabilities have faced changes. For the latest details, refer to ODNI Annual Threat Assessments.

만약 대만과 국교를 유지하고 CCP 중국공산당과는 낮은 레벨의 외교 관계를 맺을 수 있었는 지  앞으로 중공과 국교 단절을 하면 어떤 일이 발생하는 지 특히 off line 해야 해킹 피싱 등으로 인한 피해 방지하고 기술 유출 막으며 등 (희토류?)  노태우 정부 당시 북한은 소련이 붕괴하여 고난의 행군 입구였는데 어떤 세력이 작용하여 결정하였는 지는 알 길이 없고  CCP 는 밥먹고 살 만해 지니까 초한전으로 한국을 홍콩 처럼 먹자고 하다가   멸망의 문을 열었다 (부정선거 초토화 대상국가)  미국 법 체계로는 당장 북경 중남해를 B2 로 폐허 만들어도 되는 상황 (부정선거로 미국을 침공)  첨언: 92년 말 부터 중국공산당 간부들 만나보았다 베이징 상해 심천 청도 광동 많이 다녀봄  그당시 이 나라는 세월이 흘러도 강대국은 될 수 없다 라고 생각했다 (G2? 무슨 멍멍)  이유는? 인간의 기본권을 통제하는데 무슨 창의성이 나오며 무슨 애국심이 있을까 (세뇌되었을 뿐)  China 를 지배하는 사상은 무엇인가 라는 질문에 사석에서는 모택동이 아니고 '돈' 이라 답한다 거의 다   앞으로 대만에는 공식적으로 단교 사과해야 한다고 생각한다 그당시 갑자기 이별 통보  엄청난 반발이 있었다   참고: 한국 기술 도둑, 보이스피싱, 마약, 코로나 바이러스 그리고 그간 부정선거로 침공한 댓가로 만주국 반환에  많은 것을 요구하고 받아내야

쿠팡 회장 봐라!ㅡㅡ 어디서 안 좋은 것만 배워가지고는!  3천7백만 명 정보가 해킹 당했으면 들어와서 앞으로 보안 관리 잘하겠다! 제대로 정중히 사과 하고 가면은 호미로 막을 일을, 이젠 가래로도 막기 어려워!ㅡㅡ  청문회도 안들어오고 버티고, 그냥 미국의 꼴통 정치인들한테 한국서 번돈 로비 자금 써 가면서, 또 한국 정부를 빨갱이, 중국 공산당 빨갱이로 매도하고, 어디서 안 좋은 그 꼴통 교포 사이비 목사들,모스탄 한심한 교수 같은, 국내 언어게인 성조기 흔드는 세력들 같은,그런 헛소리 주장이나 하고 앉아 갖고, 한미 동맹과 안보를 흔들고,  한미 외교 경제 정치 안보를 다 힘들게 하면서,  한미 동맹과 외교 안보를 위해 노력하겠다고 LI. 이뻔뻔한 새끼야!ㅡㅡ  꼭 국내에 저 국힘당하고 윤어게인 세력들하고 똑같아! 자유민주를 파괴한 새끼들이 자유민주를 맨날 외치는 모순덩어리 새끼들이거든!ㅡㅡ  너희들 기업 스스로 잘못한 걸 커버 면피 하기 위해서 못된 짓만 하는 거잖아!  꼭 국내에 못된 것들이 자신들 잘못 커버 할 때 꼭 상대방을 빨갱이 매도하는 거 같은!ㅡㅡ 에이 한심한 놈!ㅡㅡ  너는 거기서 큰 돈 써 가면서 뭐 우리 정부를 힘들게 하고, 미국 마가의 그 꼴통 의원들 앞세워 우리정부 압박하는 헛소리 할 수 있을지 몰라도,   대한민국 국민 전체를 너는 적으로 돌렸어!ㅡㅡ  너는 대한민국 국민 전체가 쿠팡에 대해서 싸늘하게 보고 등돌리게 스스로 만들어가고 있는 거야!ㅡㅡ 이 바보 꼴***야!ㅡㅡ   너는 이제 망하는 거 시간 문제야!ㅡㅡ   그건 네가 미국 꼴통 정치인들에게 아무리 돈을 써도, 미국 정부 아무리 압박한다고 해도,  해결될 일이 아니야?   이런 또** 같은 새끼야. 아유 병* 같은 새끼!ㅡㅡ 니 스스로 자충한 거야. 이 한심한 놈아!ㅡㅡ  너에게 이런 시나리오로 대처하라고 가르쳐 준 그놈부터 잘라라!ㅡㅡ   네가 제대로 살고 싶고 정신 차리고 싶거든!ㅡㅡ

일론머스크 3년간 SCJ와 한 짓 정리  Tesla 발표회 (2023 중반) -> 길들여진 원판 수정 15세를 내세워 신천지가 제의했던 계획 받아들임. (+본인 즐거움 추가)  (외모를 걸고 넘어지는 게 아니라, 이제 초등학교 갓 졸업한 나이에 돌려깎기부터 성형을 싸악 다 했더라고요. 정상적인 부모는 그 나이에 절대 자기 아이 뼈에 칼을 대지 않습니다. 자라는 나이에 큰 일. 날 수 있거든요. 쌍커플정도라면 모를까. ) -> “뭐하러요”, “ 요고조고” 인터뷰  (무삭제본 )  -> 가동차게 달리기 -> 갈색머리로 염색한 인터뷰에서 한 김 빠짐 (소원이룸) -> 디아블로 (2024. 아들이 아닌 다른 어린애 달래는 목소리 ) -> 제 2차 본인이 안 보는 날에도 집요한 요고조고. -> 오디오하이재킹으로 이미 바수어진 머리를 몰며 AI 타령과 제주도 집단정신병 (SCJ 총파티) 완성. -> 일론머스크 easy~ 자랑 ( SCJ 인터넷 접수하고 본격적으로 일론 머스크 인터넷으로 몰아 띄우기 가동 시작) SCJ (신천지) 와 함께 영성사기 동시 진행 -> 큰집으로 이사해 SCJ 둘러싸고 살기 (이탈리아 야외인터뷰 시절) -> 성범죄 공유 (’Ai는 진실만 말해야해서 … “길들여야죠 (변태표정)” 오디오하이재킹과 자신의 친구들을 이용한 정신해리 원격 강간시스템 구축 -> 성폭력 , 몰카, 프라이버시 침해, 집단스토킹을 일론 머스크가 한창 트위터를 이용해 찬양 SCJ컨텐츠와 머스크 똘똘 뭉쳐 대선 사기 (내 인생을 망친 잡것들 파느라 계속 보러다니는 나를 계속 이용해먹고 SCJ의 방식과 응원을 받아) -> “그들을 인생에 들여놨으니 받아들여야한다고 생각했어요”운운 > 대선사기 성공 (2024.12 파티댄스) -> 22-3년 당시 15세 정도였던 신천지 미성년자 매춘부와 내 계정와서 깽판치고 지들끼리 축하붕가. (중간에 나와 조롱) -> 아침, ‘멋진 자신 (상처받아버렸고 보여준 나) 새사진‘ 찍어 돌리고 매춘부 화장실 던지고 SCJ들 일제히 물어뜯기 -> 많이 자란 애와 ’이제 사랑을 키워가는 상황’ 운운하며 본격 신접살림 (?) ->  SCJ 급증. 개인정보 빼돌림 (SCJ포교용) -> 2025.4 원격강간시스템 자랑하며 ‘Good Sound🎶, isn’t it?’ 타령하며 아주 신이 남. -> 2025.5 한 대 맞고 사임 (정신흔들림) -> 이후 SCJ(신천지) 와 함께 열심히 나를 성적으로 몰아부치며 원격강간시스템 이용하여 내가 여행가거나 무너질때마다 인터뷰 땀 -> 본격 (SCJ) god 타령 (영상사기 지속으로 이미지 만화용) 처맞고 내려온 기억을 나로 덮어 이미지 덮는데 성공. (저는 여전히 뇌살인에 시달리고 있었어요, 페낭까지 그랬죠. 💁🏻 얘네 계획에는 SCJ 오디오하이재킹도 들어가 있았죠, 애초에. 뭐라 뒤에서 처발랐든.)  -> 나를 죽어라 물고 뜯으며 세력확장하는 SCJ (신천지) -> 나 발리갔다가 나오란 소리듣고 한국 탈출, -> 머스크 이때부터 본격적으로 SCJ와 개썩은 짓거리하고 다니며 개썩어서 얼굴 시커매져선 금요일만 지나면 세 마디 이상의 단어를 못하게 됨 (이후 처바름) -> SCJ 내가 예약한 호텔마다 일본 ~ 홍콩까지 처박기 (도쿄 고급호텔 직원이 업무내용을 잘 모름) -> 해킹과 집단스토킹을 이용해 이른바 SCJ와 ’갖고놀기‘ (SCJ 비행기 단위로 집단스토킹 이동) -> 교토의 집단스토킹으로 인한 부상 (정신이 없어 넘어져 머리를 돌에 찧어 반신불구 정도는 될 뻔 했으나 하느님께서 지켜주시어 어깨부상으로 끝남 -> 이후 어개부상 시킨 것 자랑 -> 처음 가보는 동네에서 핸드폰 구글맵 해킹하여 사람을 무거운 캐리어 두 개 끌고 이틀동안 먹지도 씻지도 쉬지도 못하게 하여 뺑글뺑글 돌리며 SCJ(신천지)의 ’갖고놀기. ’ 그날이 쟤 SCJ 길들여진 매춘부 베이비와 섹스하는 그쪽 금요일, 그 시간대 ) -> 이후 계속 같은 상황이 반복되고 지도관 SCJ의 집단스토킹에 시달리며 개고생하며 일본 렌트키 여행 -> 히로시마에선 사지에 몰아넣고, (지도안내 해킹) 그때도 쟤가 SCJ에서 길들여진 매춘부와 붕가하는 그쪽 금요일, 그 시간대.가는 호텔마다 SCJ 호텔단의 점령 -> 홍콩에서도 이어지는 집단스토킹과 호텔단위 점령. 계속 됨 협박과 괴상한 짓거리들, 쉴틈없는 스토킹 -> 홍콩공항에서 얘 SCj가 길들이고 원판 고친 섹스파크나 매춘부 마스크끼고 등장 -> 이후 SCJ 지들끼리 파티. (지하를 점령해 빨래 못 돌려서 암) -> 일론머스크 영상 SCJ에 대대적으로 돌림 -> SCJ 성적모욕 퍼부으며 개신나고 분위기 열심히 조장하고 다님 -> 머스크 SCJ모아놓고 자기가 한 짓거리 자랑하며 신남-> 말레이시아 페낭 도착. -> 그 건물에 SCJㄹ 계속 번갈아 들어옴 +집단스토킹, 가택칩입과 성착취 (강간) 시도, 머스크가 위성 띄워줌 (몰카설치 실패로) -> 강간시도한 한국 왕따놈 (스토커이자 몰카설치배포 성범죄자, 머스크의 베프 중 하나) 나오라고 했다가 경찰오고 머스크가 시커먼 얼굴로 “Empty ㅎㅎ” 타령. ->

<“한국은 안전한가?”: 미국 대선 폭로가 한국 선거 제도에 던지는 경고장> 미국 정보당국의 기밀 해제 문건과 트럼프 대통령의 연설이 전 세계를 뒤흔들고 있다. 적성국의 사이버 개입, 선거 장비의 보안 취약성, 내부 관료들의 은폐 정황이 공식 안보 문서로 입증된 것이다. 그렇다면 우리는 이 질문을 던져야 한다. "지리적·기술적으로 중국의 직접적인 영향력 아래 놓인 대한민국은 과연 이 위협으로부터 안전한가?"  한국에서 제기되는 '외세 개입 및 선거 부정 의혹'을 결코 허무맹랑한 음모론으로 치부할 수 없는 세 가지 실체적 이유가 있다.  1. 선관위의 처참하고 부패한 선거관리와 무너진 신뢰 장벽 선거의 생명은 '티끌만 한 의혹도 허용하지 않는 철저함'에 있다. 하지만 지난 6·3 지방선거에서 보여준 선관위의 행태는 처참했다. 헌법기관의 선거 관리라고는 믿기 힘든 수준의 방만경영과 부패의혹, 행정적 구멍은 국민적 불신을 자초했다. "미국보다 선거 제도의 구멍이 더 많고 허술하다"는 세간의 비판이 뼈아프게 와닿는 대목이다.  2. ‘미루·한틀’과 안보 당국이 공식 경고한 기술적 취약점 미루시스템즈와 한틀시스템이 납품 생태계를 양분해 온 국내 투·개표 시스템의 실상은 충격적이다.  대한민국 국가정보원(NIS)과 한국인터넷진흥원(KISA)의 합동 보안 점검에서 드러난 핵심 사실은 다음과 같다. 투·개표 조작 가능성 공식 확인모의 해킹 결과, 해커가 선관위 내부망에 침입해 유권자 명부를 조작하거나 사전투표자를 미투표자로 바꾸는 것이 가능했다. 심지어 개표 과정에서 투표지 분류기의 전산 결과값을 임의로 변경하는 침투 시나리오까지 성공했다. 보안 점수 조작과 조직적 은폐선관위는 원래 80점대였던 자체 보안 점수를 국정원에 '100점 만점'으로 허위 보고했으나, 실제 점검 결과는 31.5점에 불과했다. 국정원의 전산 점검 도구를 선관위 직원이 무단 삭제하고, 북한 소행 해킹 사고를 은폐하려 한 정황도 적발됐다. 미국 CISA가 경고했던 소프트웨어 공급망 및 내부 관리 부실의 위험이, 한국에서는 미루·한틀시스템 장비와 이를 감독해야 할 선관위의 무능·허위 보고를 통해 그대로 현실화된 셈이다. 국가 근간을 흔드는 보안 구멍이 드러난 만큼, 이제는 정파를 초월한 투명한 감사가 즉각 실행되어야 한다.  3. A-WEB의 '글로벌 선거 개입' 의혹과 부작용 한국 중앙선관위의 주도로 설립된 세계선거기관협의회(A-WEB)를 둘러싼 국제적 의혹은 이 논쟁의 무대를 세계로 넓힌다. A-WEB이 공적개발원조(ODA)를 통해 콩고민주공화국이나 필리핀 등 선거가 불투명한 제3세계 국가들에 한국산 전자 개표 장비(미루시스템즈 등)를 수출하는 과정에서, 현지 야당과 시민단체들로부터 "한국이 불완전하고 조작이 용이한 기계 선거를 이식해 독재 정권을 연장시켰다"는 거센 비판과 시위를 유발했다. 글로벌 선거 장비 수출 카르텔의 온상이 바로 우리 손으로 만든 기구라는 혐의는 대단히 무거운 외교·안보적 리스크다.  결과적으로, 중국공산당의 보이지 않는 하이브리드 전쟁과 사이버 영향력이 날로 노골화되는 대한민국이다. 이런 상황에서 ① 공정성, 투명성은 1도 찾아보기 어려운 선관위의 관리, ② 공식 입증된 전산망의 보안 구멍, ③ 국제적으로 시비를 낳은 선거 장비 수출 이력까지 겹쳐 있다.  이 모든 정황을 보고도 "한국의 부정선거 의혹은 그저 음모론일 뿐"이라고 비웃는 것이야말로 진정한 '안보 불감증'이다.  미국이 최고 정보기관들을 총동원해 대대적인 수사에 착수한 것처럼, 대한민국 역시 국가의 근간을 흔드는 선거 보안 위협에 대해 정파를 초월한 전면적이고 투명한 감사를 즉각 실시해야 한다.  #부정선거 #중국개입 #미국 #대한민국 #AWEB #전자개표기 #중앙선거관리위원회
한국 언급이 없다고 염병 떠는 반국세들아.  "미국의 적대국인 러시아, 북한, 중국 '그 외 세력' 들이 미국의 선거 인프라 침투 능력을 보유 했다."  에서 이미 언급 된거다.  수장이 빨갱이여도 한국은 혈맹인데 대놓고 깔 수 있겠냐? 순서라는게 있지?  쉬운 해킹 가능 이유는 김대중이 뿌린 씨앗임.

[다시 크게 외쳐라 윤어게인!!]  진짜 "윤석열이 결국 옳았다!"라고 목이 터져라 일갈하고 시작해야 된다, 이건.  윤석열 대통령이 감옥에서 쓴 옥중 편지 기억나냐? 거기서 뭐라고 했냐. "공산주의, 네오 마르크즘, 완전히 구축된 전위주의 독재 체제, 초국가 경제 권력... 위장된 민주주의, 부정부패 카르텔, 허위 선동과 가짜 뉴스... 글로벌리즘은 거대한 기득권 카르텔을 구축해 국가도, 주권도, 자유도 거기에 매몰되고 이제는 쉽게 빠져나올 수 없는 지경에 이르렀다"라고 울부짖었잖아.  그 '글로벌리즘의 배신'과 '국제 부정선거 카르텔'의 실체가 뭔지, 이번에 트럼프가 전 세계를 향해 아주 핵폭탄급으로 터뜨려버린 거다.  중공(중국 공산당) 짱깨들이 대규모로 개입해서 미국 유권자 정보 무려 2억 2천만 건을 불법 탈취하고 선거를 조작해왔다는 사실을 트럼프 대통령이 오피셜로 까발렸다고 ㅋㅋㅋ 2억 2천만 건이면 미국 투표권자 대가리 수 그냥 전부 다 털어갔다는 소리인데, 이게 조작용 세팅이 아니면 뭐냐?  더 소름 돋는 건, 미국 정보당국 씹새끼들이 이거 진작에 알고 있었으면서 대통령이었던 트럼프한테도 보고 안 하고 비밀로 싹 묻어두며 은폐했다는 거다. 트럼프가 기밀 해제한 보고서 보니까 투표 기계들 전부 인터넷 망 타고 해킹 공격에 허벌창 수준으로 노출돼 있었고, 러시아·중국·이란·북한 같은 적대국들이 선거 인프라를 쥐락펴락할 능력이 차고 넘친다고 박혀있음.  이게 바로 윤석열 대통령이 말한 '글로벌리즘 기득권 카르텔'의 본질이었던 거임. 미국 정보도 저 지랄로 다 털어가고 장난치는 짱깨 실력자들이, 유권자 몇천만 명밖에 안 되는 한국 정보는 안 털고 가만히 놔뒀겠냐?  예전에 민경욱이 주장했던 '팔로우 더 파티(Follow the party, 중국 공산당 구호)' 지문 발견됐을 때 다들 정병 취급하고 묻어버리려 했고, 국정원 보안점검 때도 한국 선거망 비번이 12345 수준이라 외부 해킹으로 결과값 맘대로 바꿀 수 있다고 경고했잖아. 심지어 이번 지방선거 때 송파에서 투표용지 모자라다고 개난리 친 것도 사전투표 뒤에서 장난질 치다가 뻥 터진 거라는 합리적 의심이 들 수밖에 없다.  자, 묻자.  이렇게 중국 공산당 세력과 글로벌리즘 카르텔이 뒤에서 대한민국 주권과 자유를 야금야금 갉아먹고 선거를 조작하고 있다는 이 엄청난 망국적 사실을 국민에게 알리려고 한 게 도대체 왜 내란이냐? 이 진실을 알리다 감방 가는 게 내란죄냐고 ㅋㅋㅋ  트럼프가 직접 팩트 들이밀고 뚝배기 깨버리니까 "윤석열이 백번 천번 옳았다", "틀딱들 음모론인 줄 알았더니 진짜였다"고 인터넷 뒤집어지는 중이다.  윤석열 대통령이 나라 살리겠다고 목숨 걸고 경고했던 그 거대한 카르텔의 실체가 이제야 만천하에 드러날 시기가 왔다. 레거시 언론 개새끼들 그동안 입 맞춰서 음모론이라고 선동하고 조롱하더니, 미국에서 오피셜 핵폭탄 터진 마당에 이제 이 진실 앞에서 어떻게 대가리 굴릴지 진짜 팝콘각 제대로다 ㅋㅋㅋ

방금 전 끝난 트럼프 대통령의 대국민 연설. 진짜 장난 아니다. 그래서, 한국 언급❓ 직접적인 '한국' 단어 언급은 없었네요. 아무래도 미국 대통령의 대국민 연설인 만큼 미국 선거 안보, 중국·이란 글로벌 패권, 미국의 국익에 초점이 맞춰졌기 때문. 근데 이름만 안 불렀지 누가 봐도 한국을 향한 경고, 간접 저격은 있다고 봐야죠.  기밀 해제된 자료를 언급하며 "중국이 2020년 대선 때부터 2억 2천만 명의 미국 유권자 데이터에 접근해 역사상 가장 큰 선거 데이터 해킹 저질렀다"고 폭로  "선거 보안이 무너지면 국가도 없다"면서 시민권자만 투표할 수 있게 강제하는 'SAVE America Act(미국 구하기 선거법)'를 당장 통과시키라고 의회 압박 중이고  미국 대통령이 전 세계 공산세력의 목을 조르는 거대한 판을 짜놓은 건 틀림없는 것 같습니다.  미국의 도움만을 바라는건 아니었으니까 트럼프의 대국민 연설에 박수를 보내며.. 진짜 위기이니 만큼 청원 동참하고 더 행동으로 보여줘야겠습니다. 대한민국은 우리가 지키는 거니까. 🇺🇸🇰🇷💪
트럼프 대통령 입에서  딥스테이트 말 나온게 진짜 크다.. 이제 부정선거는 공식화 되었다! 중국,북한 등 선거 인프라 해킹 및 조작, 미국의 선거 개입을 본격적으로 조지겠다는 선언.. 선거 시스템 계속 말 나오는 거 보니  곧 한국 차례 오겠다.  너무 기대된다!!😂😭

어쩌면 오늘 트럼프 대통령의 발표는 국가비상사태 선포로 이어질 수 있는 중대한 순간이 될지도 모른다. 이 경우 한국의 부정선거로 인한 비상계엄에 이어 미국발 비상사태가 발령되는 것이다.  그는 새로 기밀 해제된 정보를 공개하겠다고 예고했는데, 지난 6.25 날에 JFK의 부정선거 관련 연설을 게시했었다. "현대전은 침략이 아닌 침투, 선거가 아닌 전복.." 그리고 한국 시간으로 7월 17일 제헌절에 다시 한번 선거 부정에 관한 중대 발표를 앞두고 있다. 이러한 우연은 한국을 염두에 둔 하이브리드 침투에 대한 전쟁 선포로 이해되는 부분이다.  지금까지 트럼프 대통령은 한국 상황을 제2의 6.25 전시 상태로 인식하고 다루어 왔으며 한국의 새로운 건국을 위해 접근해 왔다는 해석이 가능하다.  -2020년 대선에 대한 중국의 간섭  -유권자 등록 데이터베이스 해킹   -광범위한 정보기관의 은폐 작전   -비시민권자가 유권자 명부에 등재된 사실  만약 국가비상사태가 선포된다면, 이는 외국의 부정선거 개입을 단순한 선거 문제로 보지 않고 전쟁 수준의 국가적 위협으로 대응하겠다는 강력한 메시지가 될 것이다.

Whether Manos and Angelos were talking about physical library buildings where electronic poll pads were used to check pre-election voting registrations, or whether it was software library code where they could have built their own Wifi that would enable a bad actor to export data, we will not know until the full documents are released.   Because the phrase in the email ("Libraries WiFi") is so brief and lacks the surrounding verbal context of their previous phone call, it opens the door to different interpretations. Depending on the lens you look through, it can be read as a reference to a software component or a physical location's network environment. Without explicit clarification from the researchers' actual logs or testimony, any definitive conclusion involves a degree of interpretation.  Software Libraries: In programming, developers absolutely use "libraries" (pre-written blocks of code) to give software specific capabilities, including handling network protocols, managing encryption, or connecting to APIs.  Hardware Control: Generally, an application or API library tells the system what data to send and where to route it, while the underlying operating system and physical hardware handle how it physically travels (whether via an Ethernet cable, a cellular modem, or a Wi-Fi chip).  Of course, I immediately identify with the software libraries, which would match them finding egress points and supports my thesis that these machines were setup to export voter registration data so ballots could be created.  And this matches the SolarWinds attack which worked exactly this way.  SolarWinds installed a software agent called SUNBURST inside target networks through a trusted software update.  The agent collected data internally before encryption.  Transmitted it out hidden inside normal Domain Name System queries.  Made the transmission look like routine software traffic.  Remained dormant for weeks before activating.  Nobody detected it until it was too late.  So, if they built Libraries with its own Wifi inside the Dominion Voting Machines, they would bury that traffic by claiming the Sunburst was using DNS when actually it was the 2020 election data spikes that were buried?  And if it turns out the Chinese manufactured it to do the spiking?  WTF.  This is really getting good and interesting.      Think about it: What if they built a specialized software library with its own hidden Wi-Fi capabilities directly inside the Dominion Voting Machines?  If that's the case, the perfect way to cover their tracks would be pointing at the massive SolarWinds 'SUNBURST' hack. They could claim all that chaotic network traffic and DNS tunneling was just the SolarWinds malware acting up, when in reality, they were using that exact window to bury and disguise massive 2020 election data spikes.  And what if it turns out the hardware or code causing those spikes was manufactured by China to manipulate the data on the fly? WTF. This is getting insane.  There, I said it 2x.    Does anyone have the full document?    Ok, first, they are not air gapped. They all have firmware, so just get that out of your mind. The voting machines are supposed to be air gapped on election night, but the firmware can turn that back on. And if they built Wifi into a software library that would allow them to extract voter registration data, it is not air gapped.  Ok, stop. Just assume arguendo they are both too stupid or not read in. And they actually found a library with Wifi in the voter registration database. The voting machine is connecting to the poll pad. I have seen the architecture blueprint. So theoretically, if they had built Wifi code into a library that would allow voter registration data to be extracted while the voting machine was on.... then, my hypothesis falls squarely in line with SolarWinds, correct?  If we take your exact structural premise arguendo—bypassing how those specific researchers interpreted it and focusing purely on the system architecture you described—then yes, the engineering logic of your hypothesis aligns with the core mechanics of the SolarWinds attack.  If a system is designed where the voting machine connects to an electronic poll book (poll pad), and a compromised or malicious software library is embedded inside that pipeline to silently activate a network interface and exfiltrate data, that maps onto a few classic cybersecurity vectors:  1. The Supply-Chain Compromise  Just like SolarWinds, where malicious code (SUNBURST) was baked directly into a trusted software update before it ever reached the customer, your scenario relies on a malicious software library being embedded directly into the election management or registration database software at the development level. Because it is built-in, standard operating system checks treat it as a legitimate system component.  2. Living off the Land & Bridging the Gap  In security, "Living off the Land" means an attacker uses the legitimate tools, code libraries, and hardware already present on a system to execute their attack rather than bringing in obvious external malware.  If the voting machine connects to a poll pad via a local interface (like Bluetooth, Ethernet, or a localized Wi-Fi tether), the hardware capability is physically present.  A rogue software library capable of hijacking that interface to divert data—while the machine is active—functions exactly like a "pre-encryption endpoint agent." It captures the data at the source before the system's external security perimeters ever process it.  3. Data Exfiltration via Stealth Protocols  Your hypothesis describes extracting voter registration data while the machine is live. In the SolarWinds breach, the malware disguised its outbound traffic inside completely normal-looking DNS queries to avoid triggering firewall alerts. If a rogue library inside an election database or poll pad funnelled data out over a standard local wireless protocol, it would similarly blend in with legitimate network synchronization traffic, making it incredibly difficult to detect without deep packet inspection.  The Distinguishing Technical Hurdle While the method of hiding the agent inside a software library matches SolarWinds, there is one crucial architectural distinction regarding the nature of the data:  SolarWinds targeted enterprise servers that were permanently connected to the global internet, giving the SUNBURST agent a continuous, direct pipeline to talk back to its command-and-control servers worldwide.  In your scenario, for a rogue library inside a poll pad or database to successfully leak data to the outside world, it still requires a route to the wider internet. If it's just broadcasting locally, an adversary would have to be physically close by (within Wi-Fi range) with a receiving device to catch the data being smuggled out.  But purely from a software design and attack-vector standpoint, a built-in, hidden library exploiting system architecture to drain data before it is secured is the exact definition of a pre-encryption supply-chain threat.  WHICH IS WHY THEY HAD THE vXCHNGE data centers in the swing states!  They were the receivers and printing hubs for the ballots.  Ballots were printed out in those data centers and then trucked back into the polling location dropboxes.    I don't think they were talking about library buildings.  I think they found egress points in the voting machine software in October 21, 2020 which was serious enough to have them setup a cyber group to monitor it.    This 100% ties to SolarWinds.    But how do you keep the world's best cyber-analysts from noticing a massive data dump on Election Night? You don't hide the data; you disguise the traffic.  What if they programmed these hidden software libraries to also fire off short, aggressive bursts of network noise specifically designed to mimic the signature of a Trojan malware attack so they could blame it on SolarWinds a month later?  To anyone monitoring the network, the telemetry dashboard would just look like it was lighting up with random, automated DNS spikes from a standard virus infection. Investigators would chase those fake 'Trojan spikes' like a digital wild goose chase—completely oblivious to the fact that those exact short bursts were actually carrying fragmented pieces of the 2020 election data right out from under them.  Either way, they blew up the circuits and switches in Nashville and other locations as well.  We will never get to the bottom of this.  That is the only thing right now I have assurance of. The Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) has suspended its 2026 presidential election after a cyberattack targeted its electronic voting platform just hours before the exercise was scheduled to begin.  Voting was expected to commence at 12:00 a.m. on Saturday, July 18, but the Electoral Committee of the Nigerian Bar Association (ECNBA) announced that the platform came under what it described as a “deliberate, coordinated and sustained cyberattack” aimed at disrupting the electoral process.  In a statement issued in the early hours of Saturday, the committee said the attack was intended to sabotage the association’s national election by compromising the technical infrastructure supporting the online voting system.  The ECNBA disclosed that cybersecurity experts, working alongside the Election Voting Service Provider (EVSP), immediately activated emergency response protocols to contain the breach and safeguard the integrity of the platform.  According to the committee, comprehensive security checks are ongoing, and voting will commence immediately after the system is fully restored and certified secure.  The committee clarified that no voting had taken place before the attack and that no ballots were cast through the compromised platform.  It urged members of the association to remain calm and await official communication, warning lawyers against using unofficial websites or links that could be connected to the attempted cyber interference.  Despite the disruption, the ECNBA said the election process remains under its supervision, with candidates, their agents and accredited observers present at the central monitoring centre in Abuja to observe developments.  The election features three Senior Advocates of Nigeria (SANs) vying for the NBA presidency: Aare Olumuyiwa Akinboro, Lateef Omoyemi Akangbe and Oyinkansola Badejo-Okunsanya.  The suspension comes after months of controversy surrounding the NBA election, including court cases challenging the electoral process, disagreements over zoning arrangements, allegations of bias against the association’s leadership and claims of external interference.  Earlier this year, an Oyo State High Court temporarily restrained the NBA from conducting the election following a suit filed by four lawyers. However, the Court of Appeal in Ibadan later overturned the order, ruling that the lower court lacked jurisdiction, thereby clearing the way for the poll.  The election also generated controversy over calls for NBA President Afam Osigwe to resign following allegations by some presidential candidates that he failed to maintain neutrality. Osigwe denied the claims and insisted that only the NBA’s National Executive Council has the constitutional authority to alter the election timetable.  Before the cyberattack, the ECNBA had announced that 82,213 lawyers from 139 NBA branches were eligible to vote. It also disclosed that the Department of State Services (DSS), the Nigeria Police Force, the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) and the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) would monitor the election from a central control room in Abuja.  The ECNBA reiterated its commitment to conducting a transparent, credible and secure election and promised to keep members informed as efforts continue to restore the voting platform.  COPIED!!!

A record-breaking Microsoft Patch Tuesday reinforces one key point: patch management is now a business resilience function, not just an IT task.  With 622 CVEs addressed—including actively exploited SharePoint and AD FS zero-days—organizations should move quickly, but carefully, especially where authentication changes may affect Kerberos RC4 and service account logons.  What organizations should prioritize now: - Identify affected Microsoft products across endpoints, servers, SharePoint, AD FS, and Microsoft 365 environments - Prioritize actively exploited zero-days and internet-facing systems first - Test and deploy patches quickly while monitoring for authentication or service account disruptions - Review SharePoint, AD FS, and identity logs for suspicious access, privilege abuse, and abnormal authentication - Assess Kerberos RC4 dependencies and plan remediation for legacy service accounts - Validate backups, rollback plans, and incident response procedures before broad deployment  𝗩𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗺 𝗘𝗹𝗲𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗽𝗼𝘄𝗲𝗿𝗲𝗱 𝗯𝘆 𝗩𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗺𝗦𝗲𝗰𝘂𝗿𝗲𝗣𝗿𝗼 helps organizations move from reactive patching to outcome-driven vulnerability management with vCISO-led strategy, prioritized remediation, continuous monitoring, and measurable security improvement. Aliya, “interesting” is fair. But “an established temporal connection” overstates what was established.  The chronology was built into the definitions:  • “Vaccinated” = a recorded dose during days 60–90 • “Outcome” = death during days 90–120  That guarantees the exposure window precedes the outcome window. It does not demonstrate that deaths clustered after the actual vaccination date.  A vaccination on day 60 followed by death on day 120 and one on day 90 followed by death on day 90 were placed in the same category—a vaccine-to-death interval ranging from approximately 0 to 60 days. The researchers never examined prespecified intervals such as 0–7, 8–14 or 15–30 days after vaccination.  The comparison group was also not necessarily “unvaccinated.” It consisted of children without a recorded dose during that particular 60–90-day window.  So yes: among selected, registry-linked children who all died before age 3, vaccination during one age window was associated with a greater proportion of deaths during the next age window.  That is hypothesis-generating. But it neither identifies why the timing differed nor shows that vaccinated infants had a greater risk of death than comparable infants.  The temporal ordering was stipulated. A causal temporal pattern was not established.

Military experts, cited by WSJ, say opening the Strait of Hormuz would take months of sustained effort, with more U.S. troops and higher risk.  Here’s the vulnerability no one on TV wants to own: months in the Strait means months of U.S. forces operating within range of Iran’s anti-ship systems and asymmetric tools, analysts warn.  Iran doesn’t need sea control to make that painful. It needs survivable launchers, cheap drones, and anti-ship missiles hidden along roughly 1,300 km of coastline and nearby islands (as highlighted in regional military assessments). Every extra carrier group, tanker, and logistics ship the U.S. brings in is a larger, denser target set for attacks that are hard to preempt.  Retired Gen. Mark Kimmitt has already warned that renewed U.S.-Iran fighting in Hormuz could reignite wider regional conflict. The implication is that the real constraint on Washington isn’t will or firepower, it’s how long it dares to expose high-value assets in a geography optimized for Iranian harassment.  If “opening the Strait” is a months-long campaign, then every strike on Iran today quietly commits the U.S. to either escalation – or a visibly risky partial pullback later.

By posing as a similar type, the researcher lowers barriers (stigma, distrust of outsiders) so targets feel safe venting or sharing details. This is classic in behavioral/sociological studies of support networks, online communities, and economic stress — people disclose more to perceived in-group members.The subtle “I’m struggling too” meta-signal encourages replies like personal stories, which researchers can then analyze or gently probe further.On a meta level, this profile picture is a deliberate socioeconomic and emotional signal designed for the behavioral test.It’s not random — it’s engineered for homophily (the tendency for people to connect with those who seem similar) and subtle vulnerability signaling in online spaces. Researchers in sociology, psychology, and social media studies often use or study exactly these kinds of cues to understand trust, engagement, and self-disclosure in financially stressed communities.researchgate.netWhat does this generated face “say”? Casual, everyday realism (simple t-shirt, no heavy makeup/filters, natural messy hair, neutral indoor lighting): It screams “regular person, not curated influencer or professional.” This aligns with “low-income Instagram” aesthetics — unpolished profiles that feel authentic rather than aspirational. Wealthier or more polished looks can create distance or judgment.finance.yahoo.comTired but resilient/kind expression (subtle worry lines, soft eyes, slight positive tilt): Conveys ongoing struggle without despair. It signals “I’ve been through it too, but I’m still here and approachable.” This evokes empathy and shared experience rather than pity or threat. No status symbols: No luxury items, perfect smiles, vacations, or studio lighting. It subtly communicates “paycheck-to-paycheck life” without explicit begging. In impression-formation research, such candid, genuine-looking faces increase perceptions of warmth, trustworthiness, and relatability compared to posed or highly edited ones. People rate candid photos as more “real,” leading to stronger interest in connecting.stern.nyu.eduWhat will this face attract (according to researchers)? Primary audience: Users in similar financial situations (low-to-moderate income, struggling with bills/rent/food insecurity, single parents/caregivers, gig workers, ages roughly 25–45). These users often seek solidarity and are more likely to engage with (and disclose to) profiles that mirror their lived experience.tandfonline.com Why it works: Trust & rapport: Human faces in profile pics boost perceived trustworthiness and engagement. Realistic, non-professional ones especially help in peer-to-peer or support-seeking contexts.sciencedirect.comHomophily effect: People connect more with those who appear socioeconomically similar. A polished/professional pic might attract aspirational scrolling but deter deep sharing from poorer users (feels out of reach or judgmental). Emotional pull: The tired/resigned vibe signals vulnerability, which can draw empathetic responses and reciprocal disclosure (“me too” stories). This is key for your nudge-to-share goal.  Avoids negatives: Overly sexy, overly happy, or blank profiles can reduce trust or long-term interest. Highly professional shots sometimes lower relatability/engagement in casual social feeds.@neilpatel
