A car full of Secret Service agents did nothing to protect President Kennedy.  Every patriot should read “Destroying America: A Dossier on the CIA’s Control of the Government.”  Declassified CIA documents state that CIA officers are “detailed by the CIA” to be Secret Service agents.  Four of the eight Secret Service agents in the Presidential follow-up car when Kennedy was assassinated were CIA officers who had been “detailed by the CIA” to the Secret Service.  (The Presidential follow-up car, or Secret Service follow-up car as it is sometimes known, rides directly behind the Presidential limousine when the President travels.)  The highest-ranking Secret Service agent in the follow-up car was a CIA officer named Emory Roberts, who rode in the front passenger seat next to the driver, Agent Samuel Kinney. Roberts had the prime position for watching the assassination unfold, as President Kennedy was directly in front of him in the rear seat of the Presidential limousine.  His three CIA colleagues in the Secret Service follow-up car were Special Agents Glenn Bennet, Tim McIntyre, and George Hickey. Bennett and Hickey rode in the rear seat of the follow up car, and McIntyre rode on the left rear running board next to Hickey.  In CIA officer Emory Roberts’ official report, he wrote that after a bullet struck President Kennedy in the head, he picked up the car radio and told Special Agent Lawson in the lead car, “The President has been hit. Escort us to the nearest hospital fast but at a safe speed.”  Roberts “repeated the message, requesting to be cautious, meaning the speed. I had in mind Vice President Johnson’s safety,” and Roberts added “as well as the President’s, if he was not already dead.” Roberts then “turned around to wave the Vice President’s car to come closer.”  “I said, pointing to McIntyre, ‘They got him, they got him,’ continuing I said, ‘You, meaning McIntyre, and Bennett take over Johnson as soon as we stop.’”  Roberts did not punctuate his Secret Service report with exclamation points, but he was most definitely making an exclamatory statement when he explicitly told McIntyre, “They got him! They got him!”  Roberts wrote that at 12:30 p.m., before witnessing the fatal head shot, he witnessed the “first of three shots fired, at which time I saw the President lean toward Mrs. Kennedy.”  With Special Agent John Ready standing just inches away on the right front running board, CIA officer Emory Roberts clearly saw that “they got him” with the “first” shot, and he clearly saw the President react to the shot, but according to his own Secret Service report, Roberts, the highest-ranking agent in the car, sat there and watched silently for at least five seconds and possibly as many as eight seconds while President Kennedy was being shot to death directly in front of him.  Roberts said and did absolutely nothing until President Kennedy was shot in the head.  (There is an ongoing debate concerning the amount of time it took to fire all the shots at President Kennedy, but the fact is it took at least five seconds and possibly as many as eight seconds.)  Senator Ralph Yarborough of Texas, who was riding with Vice President Johnson directly behind the Presidential follow-up car, could plainly see the reactions of Secret Service agents during the assassination. Yarborough’s affidavit to the Warren Commission states that when he heard the first shot, he “thought immediately that it was a rifle shot.”  Yarborough’s affidavit also states, “All of the Secret Service men seemed to me to respond very slowly, with no more than a puzzled look. In fact, until the automatic weapon was uncovered, I had been lulled into a sense of false hope for the President’s safety by the lack of motion, excitement, or apparent visible knowledge by the Secret Service men that anything so dreadful was happening.  “Knowing something of the training that combat infantrymen and Marines receive, I am amazed at the lack of instantaneous response by the Secret Service when the rifle fire began.”  Senator Yarborough was actually witnessing the result of the CIA sabotaging Presidential protection. The CIA officers, one of whom was running board agent Tim McIntyre, knew that there would be a problem if the other three running board agents, Landis, Ready, and Hill, took action to protect the President when the gunfire began.  Kinney, as a driver, certainly could not do anything during the assassination, but if it was going to be a successful assassination, the CIA officers would have to do something about the other three running board agents.  McIntyre’s partner, Glen Bennett, took decisive action to make sure the three agents would not be a problem.  Warren Commission Exhibit 1020 contains a news article stating that Secret Service agents were “in the Fort Worth Press Club the early morning of Friday, November 22, some of them remaining until nearly 3 a.m. . . . . They were drinking. One of them was reported to have been inebriated.”  It also states that after leaving the Press Club at “nearly 3 a.m.,” the Secret Service agents went to “an all-night beatnik rendezvous called ‘The Cellar.’”  Secret Service Chief James Rowley testified to the Warren Commission about the incident, stating, “There were nine men involved at the Press Club, and there were ten men involved at The Cellar.”  Rowley testified that Bennett and the three running board agents that needed to be disabled, Landis, Ready, and Hill, had participated in the drinking and late night activity.  Out of sixteen Secret Service agents in the Presidential motorcade, CIA officer Glen Bennett and the three running board agents were the only ones who participated in the drinking and late-night activity. The running board agents were supposed to be the first to shield the President from any possible danger.  The other five Secret Service agents who consumed alcohol at the Press Club were not in the Presidential motorcade on November 22, 1963.  Rowley claimed that someone, whom he did not identify, told the agents, “There was a buffet to be served at the Fort Worth Club,” but when the agents arrived, “there was no buffet.” Later in his testimony, he stated, “and they just thought while they were there, they would have a drink.”  Rowley glossed over it with one of three different versions of the drinking incident, testifying that he “ascertained in personal interviews” that three agents “had one scotch” and “others had two or three beers.” He also testified that agents “were in and out” of the Press Club from “roughly around 12:30 until the place closed at 2 o’clock.”  But in a letter to the Warren Commission several weeks prior to his testimony, Rowley put forth two versions of the drinking incident that were different from his Warren Commission testimony.  In one version, Rowley wrote that Calvin Sutton, president of the Press Club, had said that “the Press Club has a closing curfew of 12 midnight,” but Calvin Sutton, for some unknown reason, “kept the Press Club open until sometime after 2 a.m.,” which is clearly more than two hours past the “closing curfew.”  Sutton supposedly “ordered the bar at the Press Club closed” at “about 2 a.m.,” and “as the bar was closing, a party of about four people arrived who were later identified to him as Secret Service agents. Mr. Sutton requested the bartender serve them one drink, after which the bar was again closed and the party left.”  The other version in Rowley’s letter had at least something in common with his Warren Commission testimony. He wrote that what he determined “in the course of this investigation” was that “nine Special Agents of the White House Detail were in the Press Club at various times and departed at various hours up to 2 a.m.,” which is what he told the Warren Commission.  But Calvin Sutton, president of the Press Club, admitted that four Secret Service agents were there until sometime after 2 a.m. and it was reported that Secret Service agents were in the bar “until nearly 3 a.m.”  Rowley also claimed, “The amount of beer and liquor consumed by any of them did not exceed one and a half mixed drinks, or in one case, three glasses of beer,” which is different than his Warren Commission testimony that three agents “had one scotch,” and “others had two or three beers,” and it is completely different from the claim that “about four” Secret Service agents showed up at 2 a.m. and “Mr. Sutton requested the bartender serve them one drink.”  The claim that the bar was closing when about four agents got there and that it stayed open so they could have a drink is nowhere in his Warren Commission testimony. Also, his letter to the Warren Commission did not mention anything about Secret Service agents showing up for a buffet at 2 o’clock in the morning.  His letter did not say anything at all about a buffet, but it did say they were having a “party” at the Press Club, and after the bar finally closed, they obviously took the “party” over to the “all-night beatnik rendezvous,” otherwise known as The Cellar.  After testifying that the agents did not find a buffet and were “in and out” of the Press Club until it closed, Rowley stated, “After that, some of them went to The Cellar.”  CIA officer Glen Bennett and the three running board agents, Landis, Ready, and Hill, all admitted to consuming alcohol at the Press Club and then going to The Cellar afterward. As noted earlier, they were the only Secret Service agents assigned to the Presidential motorcade who participated in the drinking and late-night activity.  Rowley claimed they went to The Cellar after leaving the bar in the early morning hours of November 22 “out of curiosity, because this was some kind of a beatnik place.”  He acknowledged that “there was someone connected with the group who was intoxicated,” but he claimed that it was just someone that the agents “ran into” at the Press Club. He claimed the intoxicated man who was “connected with the group” was not a Secret Service agent.  CIA officer Tim McIntyre wrote in his report that Secret Service agents assigned to the Presidential follow-up car were working the “8 a.m. to 4 p.m. shift” on November 22, 1963.  Chief Justice Earl Warren asked Rowley when it comes to seeing someone with a rifle, “Don’t you think that if a man went to bed reasonably early and hadn’t been drinking the night before, he would be more alert to see those things as a Secret Service agent than if they stayed up until 3, 4, or 5 o’clock in the morning, going to beatnik joints and doing some drinking along the way?”  Rowley first tried to dodge the question and did not answer it, so Warren repeated, “I say, wouldn’t an alert Secret Service man in this motorcade, who is supposed to observe such things, be more likely to observe something of that kind if he was free from any of the results of liquor or lack of sleep than he would otherwise?”  And Rowley replied, “Well, yes; he would be.”  Warren also had Rowley read from the Secret Service manual, which strictly prohibits the consumption of alcohol, “including beer and wine, by members of the White House Detail and special agents cooperating with them, or by special agents on similar assignments, while they are in a travel status.”  All such agents “are considered to be subject to call for official duty at any time while they are in travel status . . . either during the day or night when they are off duty.”  The manual clearly reflects the seriousness of violating the prohibition against alcohol, stating, “Violation or slight disregard” of these regulations “at any time will be cause for removal from the service.”  As noted earlier, Landis, Ready, and Hill, being assigned to the outside running boards of the Presidential follow-up car, would be the first to react in the event of any danger to the President. They all had to wake up early, get ready, and report for the 8 a.m. shift, just a few short hours after their all-night partying with Bennett.  Bennett’s CIA colleague, Tim McIntyre, the fourth agent on the running boards, certainly did not need to be disabled. CIA officers McIntyre and Bennett were the agents that Roberts immediately assigned to “take over” Vice President Johnson after President Kennedy was shot in the head. That was when CIA officer Emory Roberts exclaimed, “They got him! They got him!”  Secret Service Chief Rowley wrote a letter to the Warren Commission stating, “The first duty of agents in the motorcade is to attempt to cover the President as closely as possible and practicable and to shield him by attempting to place themselves between the President and any source of danger.  “Agents are instructed that it is not their responsibility to investigate or evaluate a present danger, but to consider any untoward circumstances as serious and to afford the President maximum protection at all times.”  The running board agents’ Secret Service reports lay out in detail how they were unable to perform their “first duty” of affording the President “maximum protection at all times.”  Special Agent Landis’s report states that after he arrived at Dallas Love Field at 11:35 a.m., less than an hour before the assassination, he “walked to where the motorcade vehicles were parked.”  He then stood by the Presidential follow-up car and thought it would be funny to ask Special Agent Kinney, the agent who would be driving the car, where the follow-up car was. In Landis’s own words, “I remember speaking to him and standing by the follow-up car and jokingly asking him if he could tell me where the follow-up car was.”  Landis also “walked over to Special Agent Win Lawson just to double check to see if I was still assigned to work the follow-up car as had previously been arranged.” (Landis was clearly hoping against hope that he would not have to stand upright on an outside running board.)  When the Presidential follow-up car “started moving,” Landis was standing “with my right leg on the running board and my left leg up and over and inside the follow-up car. I stayed in this position until we were leaving the airport area and remarked that, ‘I might as well get all the way in,’ and I did so.” (Landis arbitrarily decided that President Kennedy could make do with only three running board agents protecting him on November 22, 1963.)  Landis wrote that after he climbed all the way into the Presidential follow-up car, Roberts told him to “get back on the outside running board ‘just in case.’”  As the highest-ranking Secret Service agent in the car, CIA officer Emory Roberts certainly did not want to be scrutinized for allowing Landis to sit inside the car during the assassination. Roberts knew, however, that Landis was in no shape to be on guard against a Presidential assassination.  In the agents’ reports on the drinking incident, Landis admitted that he did not leave The Cellar, the all-night beatnik rendezvous, until “approximately 5:00 a.m.” But how much alcohol the agents actually consumed during their all night partying will never be known because the “Secret Service” is the only source for that information.  Landis wrote that when the Presidential limousine and the follow-up car were turning left onto Elm Street to go past the Texas School Book Depository, which would be the only building on the President’s right side, he “made a quick surveillance of a building which was to be on the President’s right once the left turn was completed.”  He described the Book Depository as a “modernistic type building,” and he wrote that when the first shot was fired, it “sounded like the report of a high-powered rifle from behind me, over my right shoulder.” (Lee Harvey Oswald allegedly fired three shots from the sixth floor window of the Book Depository.)  Landis also wrote, “There was no question in my mind what it was,” but Landis ignored the fact that Agents are specifically instructed that it is “not their responsibility to investigate or evaluate a present danger.”  He stated that after definitively hearing the report of a high-powered rifle, “My first glance was at the President,” and then, “I immediately returned my gaze over my right shoulder toward the modernistic building I had observed before.”  His report states that it was just “a quick glance” at the Texas School Book Depository, and he “saw nothing.” But he continued to violate the directive not to investigate or evaluate a present danger when, after seeing “nothing,” he “immediately started scanning the crowd at the intersection from my right to my left.”  Landis then “began to think that the sound was a firecracker,” even though it “sounded like the report of a high-powered rifle,” and there was “no question” in his mind as to what it was.  A few seconds later, “the next shot was fired” and Landis “thought that maybe one of the cars in the motorcade had a blowout that echoed off the buildings,” and Landis “looked at the right front tire of the President’s car,” at which point Landis witnessed President Kennedy being shot in the head.  Special Agent Landis’s own report makes it clear that he was dazed and confused while the President was being assassinated. His report makes it clear that when he arrived at the Dallas airport less than an hour earlier, he was in no shape to perform his “first duty” of affording the President “maximum protection at all times,” not only because of the consumption of alcohol just hours earlier, but also because of a definitive lack of sleep. Attackers are moving faster, and the attack surface is expanding. Today's threat landscape blends AI automation, supply‑chain exposure, and persistent misconfigurations — forcing defenders to change how security is operated and measured.  Top trends to watch: - AI‑assisted reconnaissance and automated exploit chains shorten time‑to‑compromise. - Vulnerabilities in core platforms and third‑party components put even minimal installs at risk. - Visibility gaps and weak runtime controls turn incidents into escalations.  Practical priorities for security teams: 1) Map and inventory critical services; enforce least privilege and immutable infrastructure patterns. 2) Automate patching and integrate runtime integrity monitoring into production. 3) Feed threat intelligence into CI/CD pipelines and incident playbooks for rapid response. 4) Test detection continuously — detection is code that must be measured and improved.  Dexmond recommends shifting focus from perimeter assumptions to continuous verification and runtime protection. Close blind spots before adversaries turn them into footholds.  #Dexmond #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #ZeroTrust

A person parked outside one daycare is physically present, visible, and limited to one location. An authorized user with access to a distributed location database can search someone remotely, invisibly, across many cameras and across time.  Those are not equivalent capabilities.  “Anything can be abused” is not an argument against safeguards. The more powerful and scalable the system, the stronger the access controls and oversight must be.  Waiting until misuse occurs and prosecuting afterward is incident response, not prevention or containment. By then, the privacy violation has already happened.

Josh is being recognized for his outstanding professionalism, initiative, and unwavering commitment to supporting our officers in the field through the effective use of technology and real-time operational support.  Most recently, during a vehicle pursuit, a firearm was thrown from the fleeing vehicle, which was spotted by Josh, as the suspect threw it out the window, watching the pursuit from an intersection camera. Through Josh’s quick thinking, he helped pinpoint the location of the discarded firearm, allowing officers to recover it. The weapon was later confirmed to be stolen and in the possession of a convicted felon. His efforts ensured a critical piece of evidence was recovered and played an important role in strengthening the case.  His impact extends far beyond a single incident. Throughout the quarter, Josh has consistently stepped up to take on additional responsibilities within the ARCC while serving as a steady, positive leader during a period of transition. He is also MJPD’s first public safety drone pilot as a first responder. His willingness to embrace technology, support field personnel, and go above and beyond exemplifies the service-first mindset that makes our department stronger.  We are grateful for his dedication, professionalism, and the difference he makes every day for our first responders and the community we proudly serve.

🔰PREPARING YOUR RUST PROJECT FOR PRODUCTION LAUNCH  You have built your smart contract. You have tested it thoroughly. Now you are ready to launch. But before you deploy to mainnet and let users put real money into your contract, there are several critical steps you should complete. A production launch is not just about code. It is about processes, monitoring, and preparing for the unexpected.  Think of it like launching a rocket into space. The rocket itself needs to be built correctly, but that is only the beginning. You also need mission control to monitor its flight, backup systems in case something goes wrong, and emergency procedures ready for any scenario. Launching a smart contract is the same. The code is the rocket. Everything else is the ground support that makes the mission successful.  👉AUDITS; Having Professionals Review Your Code  Before you launch, have your contract audited by professional security firms. An audit is not the same as your own testing. Auditors look for vulnerabilities with fresh eyes, patterns you might have missed, and edge cases you never considered. For high value contracts, get multiple audits from different firms. For smaller projects, at minimum use automated audit tools and have experienced developers review your code. The cost of an audit is tiny compared to the cost of a hack.  👉MONITORING; Watching Your Contract After Launch  Once your contract is live, you need to know what is happening. Set up monitoring for important events like large withdrawals, ownership changes, or unexpected function calls. Use tools like Tenderly or custom dashboards to track gas usage, transaction volume, and error rates. Set up alerts so you are notified immediately if something unusual occurs. You cannot fix problems you do not know about.  👉UPGRADE PLANS; How You Will Fix Bugs  Even with perfect preparation, bugs can be discovered after launch. You need a plan for fixing them. Build upgrade mechanisms into your contract from the start, using proxy patterns or modular architectures. Decide who has the authority to trigger upgrades, and make sure that authority is secured properly. Without an upgrade plan, fixing a bug might mean asking all users to move to a new contract, which is disruptive and risky.  👉INCIDENT RESPONSE; What You Will Do When Something Breaks  Despite all your preparation, incidents will happen. A bug might be exploited. An upgrade might fail. A dependency might change unexpectedly. Create an incident response plan before anything breaks. Decide who makes decisions during an emergency. Document the steps to pause the contract, investigate the issue, deploy a fix, and communicate with users. Practice the plan with drills so everyone knows their role. When an incident happens, you will not have time to figure out the process.  What step in the deployment checklist has been hardest for you to implement? Share your experience below.  #RustLang #ProductionLaunch #SmartContracts #Web3 #BlockchainSecurity

Sandra Joyce, VP Google Threat Intelligence, spent the past year arguing that disrupting adversary infrastructure has to become normal practice. She’s right.  January 2026: Google's threat intel team and partners dismantled IPIDEA, the world's largest residential proxy network. Google counted 550+ distinct threat groups using it, including state actors from China, Russia, North Korea, and Iran. Court orders killed the storefronts and 7,400+ C2 servers went dark.  Monday July 13, 2026: The EU and UK rolled out their first-ever joint cyber sanctions package against Russia.  Scott Jasper’s study of Russian cyber operations says it bluntly: sanctions and indictments have “done little to alter Moscow’s behavior.”  You can't freeze assets that were never here.  You can't arrest a GRU officer who never leaves Moscow.  Sanctioning at that scale is record-keeping, not deterrence.  What will actually degrade an adversary?   Joyce's point is that the private sector IS the infrastructure adversaries abuse. Private tech companies own the infrastructure, the platforms, apps, and networks, that these attacks run on, and they can shut an operation down directly and immediately.  The most useful thing government can do is to build the legal scaffolding that lets the private sector run these operations at scale.  My own proposal is more controversial: a licensed cyber letter of marque system (think bounty hunters for cybercrime) requiring congressional authorization, for limited private-sector offensive operations under strict oversight.   If you don’t run a hyperscaler, none of this sounds like yours to act on. It is. For defenders who aren't Google: robtlee73.substack.com/p/san…  Tell me where you land on this. And would the letter of marque idea go too far for you? I want the argument.

The corporate media and institutional liberal mouthpieces are in lockstep, working overtime to frame a recent congressional vote as a watershed moment in American foreign policy. In mid-July 2026, when 103 House Democrats voted in favor of an amendment introduced by Republican Representative Thomas Massie to cut $3.3 billion in foreign military financing to Israel, the headlines immediately trumpeted a "seismic shift" and a profound "national rift" within the ruling party. Advocacy groups and mainstream commentators quickly characterized the vote as a historic fracturing of the unshakeable bipartisan consensus that has anchored U.S. imperialism’s blank-check support for Zionism for nearly eight decades.  This narrative is not just an exaggeration; it is completely ridiculous. For socialists, anti-imperialists, and class-conscious workers, this congressional spectacle must be analyzed for what it truly is: a calculated exercise in virtue signaling and a sophisticated safety valve designed to manage domestic dissent. Rather than an authentic rebellion against the mechanics of empire, the vote represents a well-worn tactic of capitalist politicians "reading the room". By casting performative votes for a measure they knew was mathematically guaranteed to fail, these politicians aim to pacify an increasingly radicalized, anti-war constituency without fundamentally disrupting the flow of imperialist resources to a crucial Middle Eastern proxy.  A Pre-Choreographed Defeat To appreciate the theatrical nature of the vote, one only needs to examine the legislative mechanics. The proposal was introduced as an amendment to the 2027 State Department and national security funding bill. Because it was engineered by a libertarian Republican and destined for absolute rejection by the overwhelming majority of both corporate caucuses, the amendment never stood a chance of passing. It collapsed in a lopsided margin of 104 to 314.  American politicians frequently deploy this precise tactical maneuver: voting in favor of a progressive, anti-war, or popular measure only when they are entirely certain it will fail. This allows them to generate a paper trail of progressive dissent, which they can later present to their base during tight election seasons, while ensuring the underlying state apparatus continues to function exactly as the financial oligarchy intends.  As the vote took place, the material reality on the ground remained entirely unbothered by the actions in Washington. U.S.-manufactured bombs continued to rain down across Gaza, Lebanon, and the broader region. The total failure of the amendment meant that the actual pipelines of military capital, weapons transfers, and logistical intelligence flowing from Washington to Tel Aviv remained completely intact. The vote changed nothing for the victims of imperialist violence, but it provided a pristine public relations shield for over a hundred congressional representatives looking to insulate themselves from public fury.  Reading the Room: The Panic Behind the Performance The real catalyst for this sudden burst of congressional conscience is not a moral awakening on Capitol Hill, but rather acute electoral panic ahead of the upcoming midterm elections. The vast majority of Democratic voters—particularly youth, Arab-American communities, and the broader working class—have decisively turned against the U.S.-backed genocide. For nearly three years, millions of people have marched, organized strikes, disrupted logistics, and sustained encampments, fundamentally shattering the domestic legitimacy of the administration’s foreign policy.  Furthermore, the threat of primary challenges from the left has sent shockwaves through the party hierarchy. In districts across the country, grassroots anti-war candidates have actively targeted and unseated decades-long Democratic incumbents who remained staunchly loyal to the American Israel Public Affairs Committee (AIPAC) agenda.  Faced with this groundswell, these 103 politicians are simply adapting to survive. They recognize that an unyielding, overt defense of the Israeli state has become a political liability that could cost them control of the House of Representatives. By registering a "yes" vote on a doomed amendment, they throw a bone to their disgruntled base, hoping to neutralize the threat of primary challengers and keep progressive voters tethered to a capitalist, two-party electoral system.  The Tell-Tale Signs: Pelosi, Smith, and Jayapal The most definitive proof that this vote was an exercise in cynical brand management lies in the specific roster of individuals who broke ranks to support the measure.  The Pelosi Paradox The inclusion of former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi among the 103 "yes" votes should be the clearest warning sign that this is an establishment-approved maneuver rather than a grassroots mutiny. Pelosi is one of the ultimate institutional architects of modern American imperialism and a lifelong, unshakeable ally of the Zionist state.  Throughout her decades in leadership, Pelosi has consistently marshaled billions in military aid for Israel and actively worked to marginalize any genuine anti-imperialist sentiment within her own ranks. Just a few years prior, Pelosi infamously slandered organic, mass protests against the war in Gaza, going so far as to baselessly demand that the FBI investigate peaceful anti-war demonstrators and suggest, without evidence, that they were financed by Russia. For an institutional pillar like Pelosi to suddenly vote to halt aid to Israel means she calculated that the vote carried absolutely zero systemic risk. She knew the bill would fail, and she knew her vote would help soften the toxic, pro-war image of the Democratic brand ahead of a difficult election cycle.  Adam Smith’s Calculated Retraction The vote of Representative Adam Smith of Washington state—the powerful ranking member of the House Armed Services Committee—is equally telling. Smith is deeply embedded within the military-industrial complex and has spent his career rubber-stamping massive defense budgets and weapons deployments worldwide.  However, Smith’s congressional seat in Washington’s 9th District is currently facing long-shot challenges from leftist, anti-war candidates running explicitly on an arms embargo platform. Facing direct electoral pressure from his constituents, Smith cast a "yes" vote while releasing a highly defensive public statement. He went out of his way to reiterate his absolute, fundamental support for the Israeli state and its right to combat regional adversaries, framing his vote merely as a paternalistic attempt to "get the attention" of the Netanyahu government. Smith’s vote was not an anti-imperialist stand; it was a desperate defensive maneuver designed to insulate his right flank from an organic primary challenge.  Pramila Jayapal and the Role of the Pseudo-Left The participation of Representative Pramila Jayapal, the longtime leader of the Congressional Progressive Caucus, rounds out this legislative illusion. For years, pseudo-left politicians like Jayapal have served a vital systemic function for the Democratic Party: providing a radical-sounding veneer to keep left-leaning workers and youth captured within the confines of bourgeois politics.  Jayapal’s public record is defined by a history of rhetorical capitulation under pressure. In mid-2023, after correctly identifying Israel as a "racist state" during a progressive conference, Jayapal faced an immediate, ferocious backlash from the Democratic party establishment and right-wing media. Rather than standing her ground or mobilizing her base against the imperialist consensus, she quickly folded, issuing a lengthy, groveling public apology and voting in favor of a clean, bipartisan congressional resolution that explicitly declared Israel was not a racist or apartheid state. By joining this symbolic vote, Jayapal merely performs the role written for her: acting as the progressive vanguard of a party that remains fundamentally committed to empire.  full article, link below  unplugtheempire.com/post/the…

This goal is far more than just a clinical finish. It is a complete showcase of Declan Rice’s elite football intelligence, positional awareness, and his ability to execute a rapid defensive-to-offensive transition in a matter of seconds. 1. Reading the Play & Positioning (Pre-Action Phase)From the very first moment, Rice is not moving randomly. He demonstrates constant scanning and high-level game reading. He anticipates the opponent’s intended pass before it is played by observing body orientation and head movement.  He positions himself intelligently to cover the passing lane while remaining in the “shadow of the ball.” This allows him to stay compact defensively yet spring into action instantly. His body shape remains open, giving him the flexibility to react in multiple directions. This is a masterclass in proactive positioning rather than reactive running. 2. The Interception (Ball-Winning Phase)The moment of winning possession is technically and tactically excellent:Angle of approach is perfect — he attacks the ball from the side rather than straight on, preventing the opponent from shielding it. Body shape is low, balanced, and strong, allowing him to win the ball cleanly with his nearest foot. The interception is decisive yet controlled, with no reckless diving or fouling. This reflects his ability to dominate duels in transition zones while maintaining composure.  This clean recovery immediately flips the phase from defensive to attacking. 3. Transition & Ball Progression (The Most Impressive Phase)This is where Rice’s quality truly stands out:First touch is directional and purposeful. It doesn’t just control the ball — it takes him away from pressure and opens his body toward goal. He executes a quick half-turn / body rotation, instantly facing forward and scanning the pitch again. Instead of playing a safe sideways or backward pass, he makes a progressive decision to carry the ball himself into the central channel or half-space. This exploits the space created by the opponent’s disorganization after losing possession. His speed of decision-making under pressure is elite. While progressing with the ball, he continuously scans, identifies the lack of immediate pressure, and chooses the most direct route forward.  This is the modern midfielder’s dream profile: winning the ball high up and immediately becoming a threat in transition. 4. The Finish (Execution Phase)Rice maintains perfect composure and balance right until the end. He doesn’t rush the shot. He selects the right technique based on the angle and situation — whether it’s power, placement, or a combination of both. The contact is clean, the body remains over the ball, and the finish is confident and accurate.Why This Goal Is Tactically SignificantRice exemplifies the hybrid modern midfielder:Elite ability to win the ball in advanced areas Immediate switch from defensive actions to progressive play High-quality decision-making in transition Technical composure to finish under pressure  This is exactly why he is widely regarded as one of the top 3 midfielders in the world right now (if not the best in his profile). He influences every phase of the game within a few seconds — from reading the opponent’s intention to putting the ball in the net.

백악관이 공개한 내용만으로 중국이 2020년 미국 대통령선거를 조작했다고 단정하기는 법적으로 무리가 있습니다. ‘대선 조작’이라는 표현은 단순히 외국 세력이 유권자 정보를 수집하거나 선거에 관심을 보였다는 의미를 넘어, 투표·개표·유권자등록·결과 집계와 같은 선거 절차에 불법적으로 개입하여 선거 결과에 영향을 주었거나 이를 변경하려 했다는 사실을 전제로 하기 때문입니다.  현재 제기된 핵심 의혹은 중국이 약 2억 2천만 명 규모의 미국 유권자 관련 정보를 확보했다는 것입니다. 그러나 유권자 정보를 확보한 행위와 선거 결과를 조작한 행위는 법적으로 서로 다른 사실관계입니다. 유권자 명부나 개인정보를 불법적으로 해킹하거나 탈취했다면 개인정보보호법 위반, 컴퓨터 시스템 침입, 간첩·정보활동 등의 문제가 될 수 있습니다. 하지만 그러한 정보 취득만으로 투표용지가 위조되었다거나, 유권자등록 내용이 변경되었다거나, 개표 결과가 조작되었다는 결론이 곧바로 도출되는 것은 아닙니다.  특히 해당 자료에는 중국이 정보를 ‘구매하거나, 훔치거나, 해킹했다’는 서로 다른 취득 방식이 함께 언급되어 있습니다. 미국에서는 주에 따라 유권자등록 정보 일부가 공개되거나 일정한 조건 아래 판매되기도 합니다. 따라서 공개자료를 구입한 행위, 민간 데이터 업체에서 유출된 정보를 취득한 행위, 정부의 유권자등록 시스템을 직접 해킹한 행위는 법적으로 명확하게 구분되어야 합니다. 이 세 가지를 구분하지 않고 전체를 ‘중국의 선거 해킹’으로 표현하면 실제 확인된 사실보다 훨씬 강한 결론을 내리게 됩니다.  중국 정부에 대한 행위 귀속도 별도의 입증이 필요합니다. 특정 서버나 해커가 중국 내에서 활동했다는 사정만으로 그 행위가 곧바로 중국 정부의 지시나 통제 아래 이루어졌다고 확정할 수는 없습니다. 사용된 악성코드, 접속 기록, 지휘·보고 체계, 자금 흐름, 정부기관과의 연계성 등 객관적인 기술자료와 정황이 종합되어야 합니다. 사이버공격은 공격자가 다른 국가의 서버를 경유하거나 흔적을 위장할 수 있으므로, 국가 차원의 책임을 인정하기 위해서는 단순한 추정 이상의 근거가 요구됩니다.  또한 대선 조작을 인정하려면 중국이 유권자 정보를 취득했다는 사실뿐 아니라, 그 정보가 실제 선거 절차에 어떻게 사용되었는지가 밝혀져야 합니다. 예컨대 불법 유권자등록, 투표자 사칭, 투표용지 위조, 선거관리 시스템 침입, 득표수 변경, 개표자료 훼손 등의 구체적인 행위가 확인되어야 합니다. 나아가 그러한 행위가 실제 선거 결과에 영향을 미쳤거나 적어도 결과를 변경하려는 목적 아래 실행되었다는 인과관계와 고의도 입증되어야 합니다.  정보기관의 첩보자료나 정부의 공식 발표 역시 그 자체로 법적 사실이 확정되었음을 의미하지는 않습니다. 정보기관의 미가공 첩보는 수사나 분석의 출발점이 될 수 있지만, 법정에서 증거로 사용되려면 정보의 출처와 신뢰성, 수집 과정의 적법성, 원본성, 조작 가능성 등이 검증되어야 합니다. 특히 익명의 정보원 진술이나 간접 보고, 출처가 공개되지 않은 기밀자료는 반대신문이나 독립적인 검증이 어렵다는 한계가 있습니다.  형사책임을 묻는 경우에는 행위자의 신원, 구체적인 범죄행위, 고의, 인과관계가 합리적 의심이 없을 정도로 증명되어야 합니다. 국가적·정치적 판단에서는 형사재판과 동일한 증명 수준이 요구되지 않을 수 있지만, 한 국가가 다른 국가의 대통령선거를 조작했다는 중대한 결론을 공적으로 확정하려면 그에 상응하는 강한 증거가 필요합니다. 정부의 주장이나 기밀해제 문서의 존재만으로 이러한 입증이 완료되었다고 볼 수는 없습니다.  따라서 현재 확인되는 범위에서 가장 신중한 평가는 중국이 미국 유권자들의 대규모 개인정보를 수집하거나 확보했을 가능성이 제기되었고, 그것이 사실이라면 심각한 개인정보 침해와 국가안보 문제가 될 수 있다는 것입니다. 그러나 유권자 정보의 취득과 실제 선거 조작 사이에는 아직 법적으로 입증되어야 할 여러 단계가 존재합니다. 중국의 행위가 어느 시스템을 대상으로 했는지, 실제 투표나 개표 과정에 침입했는지, 선거 결과에 영향을 주었는지, 중국 정부가 이를 지시하거나 통제했는지 등이 객관적인 증거를 통해 확인되지 않는 이상, 이를 “중국이 미국 대선을 조작한 사실이 밝혀졌다”고 표현하는 것은 증거보다 결론이 앞선 해석입니다.

What worries me about Kimi, GLM, DS, MM, etc is how much of a role distillation plays in their training process.  Using outputs, and even CoTs, from Fable won’t give you Fable level intelligence. A part of distillation that’s important is capturing the probability distribution which consumers don’t have access to. Intelligence isn’t about rote learning the answers, it’s about establishing the patterns. A bit of that is carried in the probability output.  This isn’t a fully apt description but: Fable is the A student that did the hard work, Opus is the B student that understands the patterns, and something like GLM-5.2 is the C student who rote learned a fair bit. So we need a more diverse benchmark summation than ECI to capture those deficiencies.  Also, even Sol isn’t at Fable’s level of perception. I think that might only come with the increased parameter size. If Kimi is below Sol on AA, there’s no way it’s above Fable.

⚠️Data Centers Killing the First & Fourth Amendments.⚠️  Darren Blanchard, a local farmer. His offense; Exceeding his 3-minute speaking limit by just a few seconds during a highly contentious data center hearing.  “The Five-Second Arrest: Claremore’s Clampdown on Public Comment  When bodycam footage from a Claremore, Oklahoma public meeting surfaced online, it didn’t show a threat or a physical confrontation. It simply showed a man trying to hand over some documents, followed by a flat, direct order:  "Arrest him."  The man in handcuffs was Darren Blanchard, a local farmer. His "offense"? Exceeding his three-minute speaking limit by just a few seconds during a highly contentious February 17 public hearing.  The meeting centered on Project Mustang—a proposed 300-acre data center campus in Claremore Industrial Park. Tensions were already high, as many residents felt city officials had negotiated behind closed doors using nondisclosure agreements.  When Blanchard took the podium, the atmosphere quickly soured:  Blanchard was addressing local record transparency and the data center when he hit the three-minute mark. He paused briefly due to audience applause and tried to finish his point.  City Manager John Feary ordered him to stop. When Blanchard walked to the front of the room to hand his printed documents to the city clerk, police intervened.  Blanchard asked on what grounds he was being removed. Instead of a warning or an escort out, officers were ordered to arrest him on the spot. He was handcuffed and charged with criminal trespassing.  Getting the bodycam footage of the arrest to the public proved to be its own battle. A local resident who filed a public records request was initially met with a staggering $1,750 fee to release the tape. After significant pushback, the city ultimately dropped the price to $120, allowing the video to go viral.  Blanchard’s legal team has since filed a motion to dismiss the trespassing charge, which carries a $200 fine. They are also demanding that the city attorney recuse himself from the prosecution, pointing out that because the attorney was in the room, he is a direct witness to the arrest.  Blanchard, who has no prior criminal history, maintains the arrest was purely retaliatory—a move designed to have a chilling effect on any local opposition to Project Mustang.”  Credit: Dare to Know on Facebook

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of 4M São Paulo Student Records  A darkweb forum post reveals that a threat actor known as 'Confia Company' is allegedly selling a database containing personal information of over 4 million students from the São Paulo State Department of Education (SEDUC-SP). The listing, posted on July 16, 2026, claims the data was extracted directly from the API of 'Sala do Futuro,' the official educational platform used by the state's public school system.  According to the post, the dataset covers approximately 1,480 cities within São Paulo state and includes sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) for each student. The alleged exposed fields include:  *   Student ID and RA (Registration Number) *   Full name *   CPF (Brazilian national ID) and RG (state ID) *   Date of birth and gender *   Email addresses *   Parents' full names *   Complete home addresses (street, number, neighborhood, ZIP code, city, state) *   Geolocation coordinates (latitude/longitude) *   Class enrollment details  The listing provides sample records showing data for students in cities such as São José dos Campos, São Paulo, Araraquara, and São Caetano do Sul, confirming the presence of CPFs, parent names, and precise address details. The actor invites interested buyers to contact them via Telegram or Session for pricing.  São Paulo State is the largest education system in Brazil, serving millions of students annually. The exposure of such granular data, including geolocation and national identification numbers, poses significant risks of identity theft, social engineering, and physical safety threats to minors and their families. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by SEDUC-SP or security researchers.  #DataBreach #EducationSecurity #Brazil #SaoPaulo #PII #DarkWeb #StudentData

⚠️ Threat Actor Claims 605M Pakistan CNIC Records Leaked on Darkweb  A darkweb forum post reveals the alleged exposure of a massive dataset containing 605 million rows of Pakistani citizen data, combining national identity records with historical telecom information. The actor, operating under the handle "PKMKB," claims to have compiled this dataset over more than a decade by merging older leaked telecom directories and provincial agency breaches.  According to the listing, the exposed data includes highly sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and tracking details:  *   **Core National Identity Data:** 13-digit Computerized National Identity Card (CNIC) numbers, full legal names, gender, dates of birth, and family tree links including parentage. *   **Telecom Trackers:** Active and historically registered mobile numbers mapped to specific IDs, SIM card issuance dates with exact activation timestamps, and network operator data for carriers such as Jazz, Telenor, Zong, and Ufone. *   **Locational Data:** Permanent and temporary home and work addresses submitted during CNIC registrations, along with city and district routing information.  The post alleges that half of the dataset is available for free via a Telegram bot, while the full archive is offered for sale. Pakistan’s National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA) manages the CNIC system for a population of over 240 million people. While the claimed volume of 605 million rows exceeds the country's population, the actor attributes this to overlapping historical records and multi-generational telecom mappings.  If authentic, the exposure of comprehensive identity and telecom records could facilitate large-scale identity theft, financial fraud, and targeted harassment across Pakistan. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by NADRA or security researchers.  #DataBreach #Pakistan #CNIC #IdentityTheft #DarkWeb #ThreatIntelligence

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of Coinbase Customer Data on Darkweb Forum  A post on an underground forum alleges the availability of a database containing personal and financial information of Coinbase users. The actor claims the data, described as "2026 Crypto Leads," includes balance information and contact details for individuals worldwide.  According to the listing, the dataset contains the following fields:  *   Name *   Email *   Address *   Postal Code *   City *   Phone Number *   International Phone Number *   Confirmation Status *   Company *   Rent Information *   Interest Details *   Country *   Crypto Platform *   Crypto Balance  The threat actor asserts that the source of this data is a combination of internal API leaks and compromised merchant processors. The post indicates that the data is available for purchase globally, with the seller requesting direct messages for further details.  Coinbase is one of the largest cryptocurrency exchanges in the world, serving millions of users across numerous countries. A breach of this scale would represent a significant security incident for the fintech giant, potentially exposing sensitive financial profiles to fraudsters.  At the time of reporting, Coinbase has not commented on the alleged leak, and the incident has not been independently verified.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Leak of SpaceX Starshield Internal Architecture Document  A post on an underground forum alleges the exposure of an internal technical document related to SpaceX’s Starshield program, a secure satellite constellation designed for government and defense applications. The listing claims to offer access to a 22-page document titled "Starshield Constellation Technical Baseline and Architecture Description," bearing the document number SPX-STARS HLD-LIN-2026-042 and dated June 12, 2026. The file is marked with watermarks indicating "SPACE PROPRIETARY / STARSHIELD PROGRAM / INTERNAL USE ONLY."  According to the post, the document provides a detailed technical baseline and architecture description of the Starshield network. The actor claims the content covers several critical aspects of the system:  *   Constellation architecture and orbital design *   Satellite bus design, subsystems, and physical characteristics *   Payload integration and hosted payload capabilities *   Secure communications and inter-satellite links *   Ground segment and user terminal integration *   Launch vehicles, deployment strategy, and on-orbit operations *   Operational status, performance metrics, and lessons learned  The listing describes the material as valuable for aerospace engineers, defense researchers, and investors, suggesting it offers rare insights into SpaceX’s secure satellite infrastructure. Starshield is a significant component of SpaceX’s broader strategy in the space and defense sectors, providing secure communications and Earth observation capabilities for government clients.  If authentic, the exposure of such internal architectural details could provide adversaries with insights into the physical and operational design of secure satellite networks, potentially aiding in the development of countermeasures or targeted attacks against critical space infrastructure. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, and the authenticity of the document remains unconfirmed.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 New "WP2Shell" Vulnerability Enables Unauthenticated RCE in WordPress Core  Security researchers have disclosed WP2Shell, a critical vulnerability affecting WordPress core that can allow unauthenticated attackers to achieve remote code execution (RCE) on vulnerable websites.  Unlike most WordPress attacks, this flaw does not rely on a vulnerable plugin or theme—it exists in WordPress core itself.  Affected versions:  * WordPress 6.9.0 – 6.9.4 * WordPress 7.0.0 – 7.0.1  Patched versions:  * WordPress 6.9.5 * WordPress 7.0.2 and later  Organizations running affected versions should:  * Update WordPress immediately. * Verify automatic updates completed successfully. * Review web server logs for suspicious requests targeting `/wp-json/batch/v1`. * Investigate any internet-facing, unpatched instances for signs of compromise.  💡 Analyst Note: Core vulnerabilities are significantly more impactful than plugin vulnerabilities because they can affect millions of WordPress installations simultaneously. Organizations should prioritize patching immediately, as proof-of-concept exploits often appear shortly after public disclosure.  #DDW #CyberSecurity #WordPress #Vulnerability wp2shell.com/

🇧🇷 Brazilian Hospital Allegedly Suffers Data Breach  A threat actor has listed Hospital Di Camp, a private healthcare provider based in Campo Grande, Brazil, as the victim of an alleged cyberattack.  According to the forum post, the attackers claim to have obtained internal data from the hospital. While the listing provides limited technical details, healthcare-related breaches commonly place sensitive patient and operational information at risk.  If confirmed, the exposed data could include: * Patient records and personal information * Medical and diagnostic documents * Employee information * Internal administrative records * Operational and healthcare system data  At the time of writing, these claims remain unverified, and Hospital Di Camp has not publicly confirmed the alleged incident.  Analyst Note: Healthcare organizations continue to be among the most targeted sectors by cybercriminals due to the high value of medical records and the critical nature of healthcare services. Even unverified claims warrant close monitoring until independently confirmed or denied.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Brazil

🇵🇭 Philippines' Mines and Geosciences Bureau (MGB) Allegedly Targeted in Data Leak  A threat actor claiming affiliation with the "DeathNoteHackers" community has published an alleged data leak targeting the Philippines' Mines and Geosciences Bureau (MGB), an agency under the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR).  The forum post claims to include: * SQL database dump ("mgb-master.sql") * Archive containing additional MGB data ("mgb-central.tar.gz") * Internal files and records allegedly extracted from MGB systems  The actor states the leak is intended as a protest against the DENR, though no technical details regarding the alleged intrusion have been disclosed.  At the time of writing, the claims remain unverified, and neither the Mines and Geosciences Bureau nor the DENR has publicly confirmed the incident.  Analyst Note: Government agencies remain frequent targets for hacktivist and politically motivated threat actors. Even when motives are ideological, the exposure of internal databases and administrative records can create significant operational and security risks if the claims prove legitimate.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Philippines

🇦🇷 Argentine Neurology & Sleep Institute Allegedly Suffers 636 GB Data Breach  A threat actor has listed a dataset allegedly belonging to Instituto Ferrero de Neurología y Sueño (IFN), a specialized neurology and sleep disorder treatment center in Argentina.  According to the listing, the stolen dataset is approximately 636 GB in size and was allegedly obtained in 2026. The post claims the data originates from the organization's internal systems, though no evidence has yet been independently verified.  While the forum post does not fully disclose the contents, a compromise of a healthcare provider of this size could potentially expose: * Patient medical records * Personally identifiable information (PII) * Clinical documentation * Internal administrative data * Employee records * Medical imaging or diagnostic files  At the time of writing, these claims remain unverified, and there is no public confirmation from IFN regarding the alleged incident.  Analyst Note: Healthcare organizations remain one of the most attractive targets for cybercriminals due to the high value of medical records. Large-scale compromises can create long-term privacy risks for patients while disrupting critical healthcare operations.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Argentina

🇵🇪 Peruvian Educational Institute ISAM Allegedly Suffers Student Data Leak  A threat actor has published what they claim is a database belonging to Instituto Peruano Sabio Nacional Antúnez de Mayolo (ISAM), a higher education institution in Peru.  According to the forum post, the leaked dataset allegedly contains comprehensive personal and academic information belonging to students.  The threat actor claims the exposed data includes: * National ID numbers * Full names * Email addresses * Phone numbers * Enrollment dates * Academic programs * Student records * Additional personally identifiable information (PII)  If authentic, the exposure could significantly impact current and former students by increasing the risk of identity theft, financial fraud, and highly targeted phishing campaigns.  At the time of writing, these claims remain unverified, and there is no independent confirmation that ISAM has experienced a cybersecurity incident or that the advertised dataset is genuine.  Analyst Note: Educational institutions maintain long-lived identity records that are particularly valuable to cybercriminals. When combined with national ID numbers and academic information, these datasets can facilitate identity fraud, account takeover attempts, and sophisticated social engineering attacks against both students and institutional staff.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Peru

CISA confirms exploitation of critical SharePoint zero-day  CISA added CVE-2026-58644, a critical SharePoint Server deserialization vulnerability, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Microsoft confirmed that the flaw was exploited as a zero-day before patches became available on July 14. CISA also warned that attackers are chaining SharePoint vulnerabilities to execute code, steal IIS machine keys, establish persistence, and deploy malware.  #DDW #CISA #Vulnerability #Microsoft

🇫🇷 French Insurance Database with 1.3 Million Records Allegedly Listed for Sale  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a database belonging to mutuelledesmotards.fr, a French motorcycle insurance provider.  According to the forum post: * Approximately 1,338,105 user records are included * Around 160,000 unique email addresses are allegedly affected * The data was reportedly obtained in February 2025 * The dataset is distributed in JSON format  The threat actor claims the exposed information includes: * Full names * Email addresses * Phone numbers * ZIP/postal codes * Insurance-related customer records and associated metadata  If authentic, the exposure could enable targeted phishing campaigns, identity fraud, and social engineering attacks against policyholders.  At the time of writing, these claims remain unverified, and there is no independent confirmation that Mutuelle des Motards has experienced a cybersecurity incident or that the advertised data is genuine.  Analyst Note: Insurance providers maintain highly valuable personal information that can be combined with previously leaked datasets to enrich victim profiles. Even without financial data, customer contact details and insurance records are frequently leveraged in fraud schemes and credential-based attacks.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇰🇷 South Korean Robotics Company RGT Allegedly Suffers Source Code Breach  A threat actor has published what they claim is the proprietary source code of RGT.kr (Robot Global Team), a South Korean robotics company specializing in autonomous service robots and logistics automation.  According to the forum post: * The alleged breach occurred in July 2026 * The source code is being released publicly on an underground forum * The actor claims the code was stolen directly from the company * No customer database was advertised—only proprietary source code  RGT develops autonomous service robots, including its SIRBOT product line, for commercial and industrial environments.  If authentic, the exposure of proprietary source code could allow attackers to: * Analyze the software for undisclosed vulnerabilities * Reverse engineer proprietary technologies * Develop exploits targeting deployed robotic systems * Steal intellectual property and accelerate competing products  At the time of writing, these claims remain unverified, and there is no independent confirmation that RGT has experienced a cybersecurity incident.  Analyst Note: Source code leaks can have long-term consequences beyond data theft. Even when customer information is not exposed, attackers may uncover hardcoded credentials, API keys, cryptographic secrets, infrastructure details, or software vulnerabilities that could facilitate future attacks against both the vendor and its customers.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #SouthKorea

🇩🇪 German Jewelry Retailer Trauringe-EFES Allegedly Listed for Sale on Underground Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a database belonging to Trauringe-EFES.de, a German online retailer specializing in wedding rings, engagement rings, and custom jewelry.  According to the forum post: * Alleged breach date: May 16, 2026 * Database formats: CSV and SQL * The company is based in Nuremberg, Germany * Sample records were provided from multiple database tables  The exposed samples appear to contain: * Customer names * Email addresses * Password hashes * Account status and confirmation details * Billing and shipping addresses * Phone numbers * Postal codes and cities * Order- and customer-related metadata  At the time of writing, these claims remain unverified, and there is no independent confirmation that Trauringe-EFES has suffered a cybersecurity incident.  Analyst Note: E-commerce databases frequently contain sufficient personal information to facilitate credential stuffing, phishing, account takeover, and identity fraud. If the claims prove accurate, affected users should change reused passwords immediately, while the organization should investigate the incident, rotate credentials where necessary, and review systems for unauthorized access.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Germany

Armenia detains alleged REvil suspect, but identity is disputed  Armenian authorities detained a Russian tourist named Aleksandr Ermakov in response to a U.S. extradition request tied to a suspected REvil ransomware operator. His attorneys and family maintain that authorities arrested the wrong individual.    #DDW #Armenia #Revil #Ransomware

🇧🇴 Bolivia Ministry of Education Database Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor claims to have breached Bolivia's Ministry of Education and is offering what they describe as a freshly extracted production database for sale on an underground forum.  According to the post: * Approximately 6.94 million unique records are included * The dataset is claimed to have been extracted from a live production system * The actor alleges the database contains both active and historical student records covering educational institutions nationwide * The data is reportedly structured as a large JSON dataset  The threat actor claims the database contains: * Student identifiers (RUDE) * National ID (CI) numbers * Full names * Date of birth * Gender * Educational records * Historical enrollment information * Additional student-related metadata  If authentic, the dataset would represent one of the largest alleged education-sector data exposures in Bolivia, potentially affecting students across multiple educational institutions and training centers.  At the time of writing, these claims remain unverified, and there is no independent confirmation that Bolivia's Ministry of Education has suffered a cybersecurity breach.  Analyst Note: Education-sector databases are particularly sensitive because they often contain long-lived personal identifiers and records belonging to minors. Such information can be abused for identity theft, social engineering, fraud, and future credential-based attacks if the claims are confirmed.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Bolivia

🇵🇱 Polish Publisher Wydawnictwo WAM Allegedly Suffers Data Breach  A threat actor has published what they claim is the database of Wydawnictwo WAM, a Polish publishing house and online bookstore specializing in religious books, educational materials, and audiobooks.  According to the forum post: * The breach allegedly occurred in July 2026 * Approximately 71,600 unique user records were exposed * The database is being offered for public download  The threat actor claims the compromised data includes: * Customer names * Billing and shipping addresses * Order information * Customer account details * Commerce-related metadata * Additional database records associated with the online store  At the time of writing, these claims remain unverified, and there is no independent confirmation that Wydawnictwo WAM has experienced a security incident.  Analyst Note: Even if payment card data is not involved, customer databases containing names, addresses, purchase history, and account information can be leveraged for highly targeted phishing campaigns, credential stuffing attacks, and identity fraud. Organizations should investigate the claims promptly and rotate credentials or notify affected users if the breach is confirmed.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Poland

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-19 07:46 KST
🟡 위협 점수: 5/10 (보통)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 위협 환경은 WordPress Core의 RCE 취약점인 'wp2shell'과 같이 인증되지 않은 공격자가 원격 코드를 실행할 수 있는 고위험 취약점의 공개 익스플로잇이 급증하고 있습니다([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/wordpress-core-wp2shell-rce-flaws-get-public-exploits-patch-now/)). 또한, AI 에이전트 및 자동화 도구를 악용한 공급망 공격과 소프트웨어 패키지(npm)를 통한 RAT 유포가 지속적으로 관찰되고 있으며([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/seven-malicious-vite-npm-packages-use.html)), 기업의 IT 지원 시스템이나 제3자 관리 플랫폼을 경유한 데이터 유출 사고가 빈번하게 발생하고 있습니다([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207663)). 특히 CISA는 실제 공격에 악용된 취약점들을 긴급 보안 목록에 추가하며 신속한 패치를 강력히 권고하고 있습니다([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207666)).

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **Cavern Manticore**: 이란 연계 APT 그룹으로, 이스라엘을 타겟으로 하는 모듈형 C2 프레임워크를 운용 중임 ([Check Point Research](https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/cavern-manticore-exposing-iran-linked-modular-c2-framework/))
- **Scattered Spider**: 영국에서 주요 멤버들이 체포 및 실형을 선고받았으나, 여전히 기업 대상 공격의 핵심 위협으로 분류됨 ([CyberScoop](https://cyberscoop.com/scattered-spider-leaders-sentenced-united-kingdom/))
- **Inc Ransomware**: SonicWall SMA 장비의 Zero-Day 취약점을 연쇄적으로 악용하여 root 권한을 탈취하는 공격 수행 ([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/vulnerabilities-threats/inc-ransomware-exploits-sonicwall-sma-zero-days))
- **GoSerpent**: 동남아시아 정부 기관을 타겟으로 Stowaway RAT 등을 사용하여 데이터를 탈취하는 지속적 위협 행위자 ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/goserpent-backdoor-in-southeast-asia/120687/))

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **니치레이로지그룹(Nichirei Logistics Group) 해킹**: 일본 최대 저온물류기업이 사이버 공격을 받아 KFC 등 식품 공급망에 차질 발생 ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207665))
- **북한발 npm 악성 패키지**: 'chai-as-init' 패키지를 통해 공급망 위협을 가한 사례 ([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5766))
- **기타**: 한국어 트윗에서는 주로 글로벌 취약점(wp2shell 등)에 대한 대응 가이드와 SOC 분석가 로드맵 공유가 주를 이룸.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-63030 / CVE-2026-60137**: WordPress Core RCE 취약점, 인증 불필요, 공개 PoC 존재 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/wordpress-core-wp2shell-rce-flaws-get-public-exploits-patch-now/))
- **CVE-2026-25089 / CVE-2026-39808**: Fortinet FortiSandbox OS 명령어 주입, 실제 공격 악용 확인 ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207666))
- **CVE-2026-58644**: Microsoft SharePoint RCE, 실제 공격 악용 확인 ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207666))
- **CVE-2026-14266**: 7-Zip RCE 취약점, 악성 압축 파일 통해 실행 가능 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/update-now-7-zip-fixes-rce-flaw-exploitable-with-malicious-archives/))

### 5. IOC 요약
- **CVE**: CVE-2026-63030, CVE-2026-60137 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/wordpress-core-wp2shell-rce-flaws-get-public-exploits-patch-now/)), CVE-2026-25089, CVE-2026-39808, CVE-2026-58644 ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207666))
- **해시/도메인**: 이번 수집 데이터 내 특정 파일 해시나 C2 도메인 값은 직접 명시되지 않음(공격 도구 및 패키지명 위주).

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **WordPress 업데이트**: 운영 중인 모든 WordPress 사이트를 즉시 6.9.5 또는 7.0.2 버전 이상으로 업데이트하여 'wp2shell' 취약점 대응.
- **Fortinet/SharePoint 패치**: CISA에서 경고한 FortiSandbox 및 SharePoint 취약점(CVE-2026-25089 등)에 대해 즉각적인 보안 패치 적용.
- **공급망 검토**: npm 등 외부 라이브러리 사용 시 패키지 무결성을 검증하고, 제3자 IT 지원 시스템에 대한 접근 권한 및 로그 모니터링 강화.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
- **점수**: 8.5/10
- **근거**: WordPress Core와 같은 범용 플랫폼의 RCE 취

📊 Gemini 토큰: 이번 15,271개 (입력 13,775 / 출력 1,496)
📈 세션 누적: 15,271 / 1,000,000 (1.5%) | 세션 잔여 추정 984,729개

news_articles.summary: AhnLab SEcurity intelligence Center(ASEC)은 다수의 허니팟을 활용해 부적절하게 관리되고 있는 리눅스 서버를 대상으로 한 공격을 모니터링하고 있다. 최근에는 전파 기능을 수행하는 악성코드를 통해 XMRig 코인 마이너를 설치하는 사례를 확인하였다.   공격 사례에서는 ShellBot 악성코드뿐만 아니라 MIG LogCleaner, XHide 같은 도구들이 사용되었다. 공격자는 Go 언어로 제작된 다운로더 및 전파 악성코드를 제작해 사용하였으며 이외에도 Shc(Shell Script Compil
